### power of the Lord, demons vs. devotees, fearlessness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=254
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So a demon may become so powerful, but in front of the Lord, in front of Vishnu, in front of Krishna, they are nothing."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a snake charmer and a cobra to illustrate how even the most powerful demons are insignificant before the Lord, emphasizing Varahadeva's unperturbed nature in the face of Hiranyaksha's power.

Because he wanted to show off. So then he approached Varanadeva and he said, I heard you are very powerful, why don't you fight with me? So Varanadeva said, I am old, I am retired, please leave me Baba, don't fight with me, don't worry, soon there will come one person, Vishnu will come, he will take care of you. So from that time, he was searching, where is Vishnu, where is Vishnu, where is Vishnu? So when he came across finally Varanadeva, Vishnu in the form of Varanadeva, so he was thinking that, ah, I am so powerful, I will finish off this Vishnu also, what is this Vishnu in front of me? So until that point of time, nobody had dared to stand up and talk to him even. So when he approached Varanadeva and started insulting him, Varanadeva immediately gave it back to him. So he got angry, how can somebody talk to me like this? Shaila Prabhupada is saying here, the Bhagavatam is saying here, that in the purport, Shaila Prabhupada is saying, that a cobra may be very fierce for an ordinary person. Like I said, if a cobra were to enter into this room, what would happen? Everyone would start running here and there, right? No one will sit here. But if there is a snake charmer, among the monsters, what he will do, he will not fear, he will immediately take out his that he blows, and he will immediately start chanting his mantras and he will subdue the serpent. Simply catch it with his hand and he will take it and go away. Isn't it? Just like in Vandavan, because we are in the midst of open fields, sometimes snakes come into our campus. So when snakes come, then all the staff, everybody starts running around and everybody is yelling and shouting and all that, and we get some snake charmer. So when the snake charmer comes, everybody tells him, see, it's there in that bush, it is sitting there. So he will go there, he will put his hand, take it and he will have a basket, he will put in that, he will take and he will go. And you pay him something for that. So snake charmer is not afraid of the snake. For him, what is there, it is a plaything. So similarly, Hiranyaksha, a demon, might sometimes become very powerful. He may become extremely powerful. Just like this Hiranyaksha, he is so powerful, he may become extremely powerful. Just like this Hiranyaksha, he is so powerful, even the devatas, none of the devatas were courageous enough to stand in front of him. Even Varanadeva said, no, I don't want to fight with you. So a demon may become so powerful, but in front of the Lord, in front of Vishnu, in front of Krishna, they are nothing. So Varahadeva is not perturbed by Hiranyaksha's strength or his power or his reputation.

### source of power, devotee harassment, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So no matter how powerful a demon may become, for the Supreme Lord, he is nothing."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that all power comes from the Lord, using the example of Prahlad Maharaj and Hiranyakashipu, and emphasizes that even when demons harass devotees, the Lord will ultimately vanquish them.

Varahadeva is completely unperturbed because the Lord is the supreme proprietor of everything. He is the most powerful, he is all-powerful. And in fact, all power is coming from him. If the demon is powerful, where is that power coming from? Just like when Hiranyakashyapu, he was trying to kill Prahlad Maharaj, his own son, five-year-old son. Just like when Hiranyakashyapu, he was trying to kill Prahlad Maharaj, his own son, five-year-old son. He tried various means to kill him. Put him into a pit full of snakes, threw him down from the top of a cliff, tried to trample him with elephants, all of that failed. And when that failed, Hiranyakashyapu started getting afraid. And he questioned Prahlad Maharaj, where are you getting this power from? None of the devatas can stand in front of me, you are just a five-year-old kid, and all my attempts to kill you are failing. How have you become so much powerful? So Prahlad Maharaj replied, whatever is the source of your power is also the source of my power, which is Vishnu. So when he heard that, Hiranyakashyapu could not tolerate. That is how he said, where is your Vishnu, show me, is he in this pillar? And we know the rest, what happened. So, no matter how powerful a demon may become, for the Supreme Lord, he is nothing. He is nothing. And these demons, when they become powerful, they try to harass devotees. So sometimes devotees become perturbed that this demon is there, he is attacking, he is creating so much harassment for us. Just like Hiranyakashyapu, so much he harassed Prahlad Maharaj. And Prahlad Maharaj was very disturbed, but he pacified himself by remembering Vishnu and Krishna. By chanting Krishna's holy name, he managed to calm himself. So similarly, in all of our devotees' lives, there will be circumstances where we will see some demon become very powerful. And he will harass devotees. It may, the harassment may last for several years. But finally, when the Lord decides to vanquish him, then nobody can come in the way.

### divine will, Lord's protection, power of devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rakhe Krishna Mareke, Mare Krishna Rakheke. Rakhe Krishna, if Krishna wants to protect someone, Mareke, who can kill him?"
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Ravana's defeat by Lord Ramachandra, highlighting that even Lord Shiva could not protect Ravana because the Lord had decided to end him, illustrating the principle that if Krishna wants to protect someone, no one can kill them, and vice versa.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada is mentioning the pastime of Ravana. Ravana was another demon who was so powerful. He was so powerful that he wanted to build a staircase directly to heaven. He was thinking, what is all this Punya, Girnya and all you have to go to heaven? I will build one staircase, let everyone go. What, only devatas should enjoy heaven or what? So he started building a staircase to heaven. He was so powerful. And he was a great devotee of Lord Shiva. And because of pleasing Lord Shiva, he had become very powerful. So when the battle between Ravana and Lord Ramachandra was going on, the fight was raging. And on one side, Ravana, on the other side, Ramachandra. And arrows were being exchanged, weapons were being aimed at each other. The fight was going on. And one by one, slowly all of Ravana's weapons were getting destroyed. So his chariot got destroyed, his horses were destroyed, his charioteer was finished, his flag was cut off, everything was finished. And then his bow also broke. So then Ramachandra fixed his last bow, last arrow on his bow. And he aimed it at Ravana. And Ravana knew, this is the end. Now, I cannot protect myself. So he immediately remembered Lord Shiva. And he started calling out to Lord Shiva. Go Shiva, please protect me, please save me. So Lord Shiva was sitting in Kailasa. Sitting with Parvati Devi. So Lord Shiva heard Ravana's cry. Ravana's cry reached up till there. But Lord Shiva was unmoved. He was silently sitting. So Parvati Devi, naturally because she is Lord Shiva's wife, so she has a... She has that little bit of familiarity with him. So she said, What is this you are sitting here, your devotee is crying. Go, no, do something. Just like wives will tell husbands, No, what useless fellow sitting there, do something. No, like that. No, like that Parvati Devi will tell, What is this you are sitting here, your devotee is crying. Why don't you go and do something? So Lord Shiva replied, Devi, what can I do? Supreme Lord Ramachandra has decided to kill him. What will I do going to there? The Lord has already decided, I cannot do anything, sorry. So Ravana was crying and crying for Lord Shiva's protection. But in the meantime, Ramachandra fixed his arrow, released it and Ravana was finished. So even Lord Shiva refused to protect Ravana because he knew that now that Ramachandra Bhagavan, he has decided to kill Ravana, even I cannot protect him. Srila Prabhupada used to often quote that saying, Rakhe Krishna Mareke, Mare Krishna Rakheke. Rakhe Krishna, if Krishna wants to protect someone, Mareke, who can kill him? Just like Krishna wanted to protect Prahlad Maharaj, even Hiranyakashipu could not do anything. And on the other hand, Mare Krishna, if Krishna wants to kill someone, just like Ramachandra decided to kill Ramana, Rakheke, who can protect that person? Nobody can protect.

### humility, anger, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=782
**Verse References:** BG 3.62
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the source of anger amongst non-devotees is the desire for sense gratification."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the anger of the Lord, which is for the welfare of living entities, with the anger of demons, which stems from frustrated desires for sense gratification, as explained in the Bhagavad Gita.

So, the demons become very puffed up by their own power and strength, thinking that they are all powerful. But devotees, as devotees, we should not become puffed up whenever the Lord invests certain power in us for executing certain services. So as devotees, devotees are always humble and they know that the source of my strength and power is Krishna, is Vishnu. So they always treat that power which is invested in them by Krishna with lot of humility. Another thing that we can notice here is that Varahadeva also became angry and Hiranyakashipu also became angry. Both of them became angry. So what is the difference between the Lord and the demon? So the difference is that when the Lord becomes angry, his anger is for the welfare of the living entity. The Lord for the welfare of the demons, he becomes angry with them. So that by his anger, he can punish them and by that punishment they become purified of their offenses and they can become liberated. Whereas, the source of anger for the asuras, it's something else. Actually, Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 62nd verse, 62nd and 63rd verse, Krishna explains what is the source of anger for the non-devotees. So Krishna says in the 62nd verse, While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them and from such attachment, lust develops and from lust, anger arises. So the source of anger amongst non-devotees is the desire for sense gratification. So because of the desire for sense gratification, when a person contemplates on the objects of the senses, he develops attachment for them, he wants to fulfill his desire to gratify his senses. And then when he, and out of that and from such attachment, when he lusts after satisfying that desire, eventually that lust becomes frustrated. He is not able to fulfill that lust to the fullest extent. Then because of that frustration, anger arises.

### anger, delusion, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=949
**Verse References:** BG 2.63, BG 2.58
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "From anger, delusion arises and from delusion, bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost. And when intelligence is lost, one falls down again into the material pool."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the consequences of anger, leading to delusion and loss of intelligence, and contrasts Dhamma (sense control) with Shama (mind control), emphasizing the importance of both.

Now when a normal conditioned soul becomes angry, Krishna explains further in the next verse what actually further happens, what does that anger lead to? From anger, delusion arises and from delusion, bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost. And when intelligence is lost, one falls down again into the material pool. So we can see here, Krishna is describing the danger of becoming subjugated by anger. Actually in fact, in the little previous to this, in the 58th verse, Krishna mentions One who is able to withdraw his senses from sense objects, as the tortoise draws his limbs within the shell, is to be understood as truly situated in knowledge. So Krishna in the 58th verse, he spoke about Dhamma, controlling the senses. That we all have to control our senses. The senses cannot be freely left to enjoy the sense objects. Now Dhamma entails gross control of the senses. Whereas in the 62nd and 63rd verse, Krishna is speaking actually of Shama, controlling the mind. One is the force of the senses, which impels us to enjoy the sense objects. And the second is the force of the mind. The mind generates certain desires. So if the mind is uncontrolled, then it finally leads to anger, and anger leads to further entanglement in this material world. So, Shama and Dhamma. Dhamma is important, Shama is also important.

---
### sense control, mind control, devotional practice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1074
**Verse References:** BG 2.60
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the need for both sense and mind control, noting that while devotees often practice Dhamma (sense control) through following regulative principles, Shama (mind control) is often overlooked, leading to frustration and loss of interest in Krishna consciousness.

If we do not control our senses, if Dhamma is not practiced, Krishna says what happens in the 60th verse. He says, The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them. So even a knowledgeable person, even a person who is a person of discrimination, not an ordinary person, a person of discrimination, a learned person, an intelligent person, a person who is a gyani, even such a person, if his senses are not controlled, what will happen? He will be led to destruction. So, one is Dama, another is Shama as we read in the 62nd and 63rd verses, if a person does not practice Shama, he may very well control his senses, but then, if his mind is not controlled, then that will eventually again lead him to destruction. So both Dama and Shama are important. Now, in the case of devotees, what happens is Dama is very easily practiced. We follow the four regularity principles strictly, then automatically Dama is practiced. And, but then Shama is something which we consciously, we may miss out in practicing. Dama is very conscious, we will consciously follow. I should avoid onion, garlic, I should not eat meat, I should eat only Krishna prasadam, very easily we can follow. But Shama is something which many times may miss our attention, that we have to control the mind also. On one level, controlling the mind means we do not allow the mind to dwell on material sense objects. On a higher plane, Dama also means that we cannot allow the mind to think whatever it wants. Sometimes devotees get carried away, that no according to me it should be like this, according to me we should do like this. And then when things don't happen according to the way they are thinking, they become frustrated. And they start thinking, what is all this, I thought I came to devotional service thinking that everything will be nice here, but there are so many problems here, this is not okay, that is not okay. And finally the person will lose his interest in Krishna consciousness.

### mind control, desire for recognition, humility in service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because the Brajavasis, they have no desire other than to please Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker warns against the uncontrolled mind generating thoughts that seem like devotional service but are driven by a desire for recognition, and contrasts this with the mood of the Brajavasis, who are solely focused on pleasing Krishna, regardless of the size or visibility of the service.

So, in the case of devotees, we have to be very careful to practice this Shama, this control of the mind. Sometimes this uncontrolled mind will generate some thoughts which will seem almost like devotional service, it will look like devotional service only. Sometimes devotees think, no I should do something big for Krishna and we give them some service, what is this small service you are giving me, I want to do something big, give me some bigger service. So, actually if one has such a mentality, even if one becomes a PR devotee, one will not be able to have permanent residence in the spiritual world. One will have to keep coming back to that. One will have to keep coming back to the material world. Because if we have that desire, I should do something big for Krishna. Krishna will say, okay, you continue doing that in the material world, you be there and you keep doing big things. Such people will generally become devatas. They are pure devotees, but they become devatas because they have some desire to serve Krishna in some big capacity. So Krishna says, okay, remain in the material world and do big things. So, a devotee of the highest order, will generally, will not have this kind of thought. If we look at the Brajavasis, why are they considered the topmost devotees, even above the devatas? Why? Because the Brajavasis, they have no desire other than to please Krishna. If Krishna is pleased by my doing some small service, I will do that small service. I will be happy doing that small service. If Krishna wants me to do some big service, then I will do that big service. I am not attached to doing something big or small, I am attached to pleasing Krishna.

### surrender, pleasing Krishna, example of Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Make me a success or a failure as you wish."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the mood of a topmost devotee through Prabhupada's example, emphasizing his prayer for Krishna to make him a success or failure as He wishes, highlighting the importance of surrender and prioritizing Krishna's pleasure above all else.

This is the mood of the Brajavasis. And we can see that very strongly reflected in Prabhupada's life also. When Prabhupada was going to the US on the Jaladuta, he wrote the poem, So there, Prabhupada says, he prays for Prabhupada, he prays to Krishna, Prabhupada prays to Krishna, saying that, you have brought me to this land of the mlecchas and yavanas I do not know whether I will be able to convince them about Krishna consciousness. And then Prabhupada says, make me a success or a failure as you wish. You see, Prabhupada did not pray, Krishna I have come here to serve you, please make me a big success. Prabhupada is saying, make me a success or a failure as you wish. Prabhupada says, make me a success or a failure as you wish. And Prabhupada says, make me dance, make me dance, make me dance. You see, this is the mood of a devotee of the topmost order. He is not attached to doing something big or something small. He just sees what Krishna wants. If Krishna is happy by my becoming a failure, I will become a failure. If Krishna is happy by my going to Naraka, I will go to Naraka. If Krishna wants me to go to Svarga, I will go to Svarga. Doesn't matter where I go, the central point is pleasing Krishna. And this is the mood of devotees Therefore, those devotees in Goloka Vrindavan are always respected everywhere. They are glorified by all devotees, everyone glorifies them, they respect them, they look up to them. They aspire to become like them. This is the standard of the highest order of devotees.

### satisfying Krishna, humility in service, mind's tendencies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1452
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the smallest thing, just cleaning the floor, you do it nicely, it's enough. Krishna and Pravupa is pleased."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of satisfying Krishna and Prabhupada in whatever service is given, even the smallest tasks, and cautions against the mind's tendency to seek attention and praise through big services.

And the same thing is what Srila Prabhupada taught us, and the same thing Prabhupada demonstrated in his life also. So we have to, we as devotees, following in Srila Prabhupada's footsteps, following the example that Srila Prabhupada said, we should try to satisfy Krishna and Srila Prabhupada in whatever service we are given. It's not that we have to do something really big to satisfy Even the smallest thing, just cleaning the floor, you do it nicely, it's enough. Krishna and Pravupa is pleased. If Pravupa and Krishna are pleased by my cleaning the floor, I'll keep cleaning the floor all throughout my life, no problem. This is the real mood of a devotee and one has to train one's mind to accept this mood and practice this mood. Otherwise mind always wants to do something which will gain lot of attention, lot of puja, pratishtha, labha. So if I do some big service, many people will come and congratulate me, many people will come and praise me, that is very pleasing for me. Therefore I want to do big service. So the mind always tends towards these things. So actually in our condition stage, if we think that I want to do something big for Krishna, actually the motivation is not pleasing Krishna. The motivation is I want to do something big so that everybody will appreciate, people's attention will be there. Right now I am doing something nobody comes to know about it. What is this? I am going inside the something I am scrubbing, nobody knows, nobody sees, nobody appreciates. So that's not the point. So we should be careful. So this will look almost like Krishna consciousness. We are serving Krishna only, what is wrong? But actually behind that there is another thing going. So we have to be careful about this, what we think, we have to control, we have to train the mind to be satisfied

### satisfaction in service, material duties, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1575
**Verse References:** BG 3.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Until and unless we come to the point where one is completely satisfied in performing whatever duties are assigned to one by one spiritual master, then one cannot, until that point one cannot be free from the obligations to perform one's material duties."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees in Kali Yuga primarily serve Krishna, but if they don't find full satisfaction in their assigned devotional service, they become liable for not performing their material duties, as described in the Bhagavad Gita.

Actually Krishna explains in the third chapter of the Bagavat Gita, I will read that verse. So for a person who is engaged in devotional service, what happens is we devotees, we do not practice any other duties that we are supposed to perform according to the Vedas. According to the Vedas we have so many rnas to fulfill, rnas to parents, rnas to one's forefathers, rnas to the devatas, rnas to the teachers, rnas to the rishis, rnas to the rishis, so many people, we have so many kinds of rnas. But then what happens is we don't follow any of those, actually in Kali Yuga nobody follows any of those duties. Instead, it is said that in Kali Yuga we have to simply serve Krishna. Right? Now if we don't perform our duties which are enjoined in the scriptures, actually one becomes liable to liable for the reactions to those sinful activities. It is considered a sinful activity not to perform one's duties. Now as devotees what happens is if we do not find full satisfaction in performing one's devotional service whatever is assigned to us, then actually we will also become liable for not performing those material duties. That is why Krishna says means a person does not have to perform those duties. Who is the person who does not have to perform those duties? This is the seventeenth verses. One who is taking pleasure in the self, number one. Number two, one who is illumined in the self. And number third, one who rejoices in the self, number three, and is satisfied with the self only, fully satiated, for him there is no duty. So, until and unless we come to the point where one is completely satisfied in performing whatever duties are assigned to one by one spiritual master, then one cannot, until that point one cannot be free from the obligations to perform one's material duties. So as devotees we should train our mind to be satisfied, to take satisfaction only in performing the assigned duties to us in whatever service we are doing. If we are not feeling that satisfaction, then we have to understand that I am going to get the reaction for all, for not performing all my material duties.

And when our mind is not engaged in trying to satisfying the sense objects, there is no question of frustration of one's attempts leading to Krodha or anger. ### service, devotional service, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Desiring to serve Krishna in some big way, it is not that it is not devotional service."
**Summary:** In response to a question, the speaker clarifies that desiring to serve Krishna in a big way is still devotional service, but it's not the highest order, which involves serving Krishna according to His desire.

A devotee is denying some you said that if a devotee is denying some services, discriminating the services, and in the future he may become a pure devotee, you mentioned. So how does the devotee who denies, discriminates the services, becomes a pure devotee? I didn't get the question. What was that? You said in the class, if a devotee is denying the menial services, want to do some big services, so in the future if he becomes a pure devotee also, he will become Devata in the material world. So how does a devotee who denies the, discriminates the services, and he can become a pure devotee? Well, the point is that, desiring to serve Krishna, in any way, is also devotional service only. It's not that it is not devotional service. If somebody desires, I should serve Krishna in some big way, it is not out of the ambit of devotional service. Because of one's past conditioning, one may have that ambition to do something big. So you, you dovetail that desire in doing something big for Krishna. Nothing wrong in that. But then, one has to go beyond even that. One has to, one has to go to a stage, where one will be simply interested in, serving Krishna's desires, whether as a success or a failure. So, such a devotee, when he becomes completely liberated, he will go to the supreme abode. He will go to the Goloka Vrindavan. So that is the point. So, desiring to serve Krishna in some big way, it is not that it is not devotional service. So that is also devotional service only, but it is not devotional service of the highest order. The main point is, when, when, higher than that is, to desire to serve Krishna according to his desire. That is more, that is even higher than desiring to serve Krishna in some big way. Is that okay? Any other?

### self-realization, satisfaction, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1941
**Verse References:** BG 3.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By serving Krishna, everything else is performed."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of 'taking pleasure in the self' (Atmarathi, Atmatruthah, and Atmanyeva Santoshra) as understanding oneself as a spirit soul, engaging in spiritual pursuits, and finding complete satisfaction in serving Krishna.

Yes, please. Taking pleasure in the self, what it actually means. When we say taking pleasure only in the self, what it means is that, one, like Prabhupada is mentioning here, in the purport to that 17th verse, I will read that. Actually, those three things, that Prabhupada mentions, Krishna mentions, Atmarathi, Atmatruthah, and Atmanyeva Santoshra. These three are connected. So, Atmarathi means, a person, when a person engages in, when a, when a person, Atmarathi means one who takes pleasure only in the self. A person who is fully Krishna conscious, and is fully satisfied by his acts in Krishna consciousness, no longer has any duty to perform. Due to his being Krishna conscious, all impiety within is instantly cleansed, and effect of many many thousands of Vijnaya performances. So actually Atmarathi means, a person who understands that, he is not the body, he is a spirit soul. So he is interested only in spiritual subject matter. He is not interested in satisfying his senses. Right? So he is not interested in searching after the sense objects. He is more interested in understanding, he is more interested in understanding about himself, in spiritual subject matter. So such a person is said to be, taking pleasure only in the self. And, when one, engages in, in devotional service to Krishna, with that understanding, that he wants to, with that, keeping away the desire for sense gratification, then what happens is, by such clearing of consciousness, one becomes fully confident of his eternal position, in relationship with the Supreme. His duty thus becomes self-illuminated. So, Atma-tripta, Srila Prabhupada is translating, Atma-tripta as, one who is illumined in the self. So illumined in the self meaning, when one is engaged in understanding, when one is engaged in spiritual pursuit, and one engages in service to Krishna, one understands that, Oh! I am a servant of Krishna. This is my true position. So, such a position is called, self-illuminated, means he understands that he is a servant of Krishna, he is a part and parcel of Krishna, and he has to serve Krishna. And such a person, Atma-santushtaha, he is satisfied with the self only. Meaning, he is fully satiated, meaning he is satisfied, so by, first is he, he takes, he does activities only, related to spiritual subject matter. He is not interested in sense gratification. That is Atmarathi. And as an Atmarathi, when one understands that I am, I have to serve Krishna, I am a part and parcel of Krishna, then he becomes self-illuminated. Atma-tripta. And when in, when, when on that platform of understanding, I am part and parcel of Krishna, one is completely satisfied by serving Krishna. Oh! I am part and parcel of Krishna, I do service for Krishna, I am fully satisfied. He doesn't, he has come to a final conclusion. He is no more searching. There is no more search. What am I supposed to do? What are my duties? Nothing. This is the final thing. By serving Krishna, everything else is performed. So that is the level of satisfaction that a person feels. So then, that person is called an Atma-santushta. So that is a person who is satiated only in, only with the Self. So one is taking pleasure in the Self, one is illumined in the Self, and finally, one is satisfied with the Self. So this is, these are the three things. It's a progressive state.

## AAQ: Can Only Full-Time Devotees Become fully Krishna Conscious? | HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Krishna consciousness, devotional service, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsHhWWbyW4E?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "No matter what condition we are in, we can become Krishna conscious."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that Krishna consciousness is possible for everyone, regardless of their material circumstances, using Bhaktivinoda Thakur as an example.

This is the question: In Kali Yuga, is only full-time devotee is possible for Krishna consciousness till death, where we can remember Krishna at the time of death? That's not true. The point is that if we look at the example of great Acharyas like Bhaktivinoda Thakur, he was engaged in government service, but still he was Krishna conscious. Of course, he is a pure devotee, he is a great Acharya, we cannot compare with our own condition. But Bhaktivinoda Thakur by his example, he established that no matter what condition we are in, we can become Krishna conscious. We can remain in pure devotional service irrespective of what material engagement we have.

---
### surrender, devotional service, activity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsHhWWbyW4E?t=55
**Verse References:** BG 9.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whatever we do, we have to surrender to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna instructs in the Bhagavad Gita that all actions should be surrendered to Him, and that one's material situation does not necessitate giving up devotional service.

So Krishna tells in the Bhagavad Gita, yad karośi, yad aśnāsi, yad juhośi, dadāsi, yad tapasya sikaunte, yad kurusva madharpanam, whatever we do, we have to surrender to Krishna. And that's why we have to give up our devotional service. Sthane sthitaha. Sthane sthitaha, wherever whatever material situation we are in, we need not give it up. If we are able to give it up and dedicate ourselves fully to our spiritual master's mission, that's the best thing, definitely. But if due to some commitments, some considerations, some opposition, we are unable to dedicate ourselves, still in whatever condition we have, it's not possible to give up. So we are not able to give up our devotional service, we are not able to give up our spiritual service, we are in, we can render devotion and service to Krishna, we can remain Krishna conscious and at the time of death by the mercy of the Guru, by the mercy of a pure devotee, spiritual master which is Srila Prabhupada, by his mercy we will be able to remember Krishna and go back to Godhead. So remembering Krishna at the time of death and going back to Godhead is not dependent upon my status. My status as a full time devotee, congregation devotee, folk devotee, Grihastha, Brahmachari, Sanyasi, these are all material considerations, material situations. According to our body, according to the circumstances we may take up a certain status or position in life because everyone has to have some, the material world is such that you have to have some status. Either as a man or as a woman. Either as a married person or as a bachelor or as a full time devotee or working outside. You have to do something.

---
### activity, karma, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsHhWWbyW4E?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we start attributing the result to ourselves, then you are on the material platform."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that activity is inherent to the soul, and the key is to avoid attributing the results of our actions to ourselves, which keeps us on the material platform.

Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita that the nature of the living entity is to be active. And therefore Krishna says, you cannot remain inactive because if you are inactive you cannot even maintain your body. So activity is the characteristic of the soul, soul cannot remain inactive. Now in what capacity he acts in the material world, as a Brahmachari, as a Grihastha, as a folk boy, as a congregation devotee, as a full time missionary, whatever activities you do in whatever condition, you have to situate yourself somewhere and carry out some activity. You cannot remain inactive. The point is, what do we do with the result of those activities? If I am a full time missionary, then I cannot remain inactive. If I am a full time missionary, but I am thinking, oh this activity I am doing because of this so much this one result is coming is all because I have worked hard for it. I worked hard, therefore so much donation came to the temple. I worked hard, so many people became devotees of Krishna. When we start attributing the result to ourselves, then you are on the material platform. So, to whom do we attribute the result? No matter in what condition we are in, no matter what status we are in, no matter what is our field of activity, that actually determines whether we receive the mercy of Guru and Krishna and we are able to remember Krishna at the time of death and we are able to become free from the cycle of birth and death and go back to Krishna Loka or not. So it's not a matter of being a full time devotee or not, it's a matter of receiving the mercy of Guru and Krishna.

---
### mercy, consciousness, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsHhWWbyW4E?t=291
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ultimately it is the consciousness of that individual which determines whether it goes back to Godhead or not."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that while dedicating oneself fully to Krishna's mission is advantageous, ultimately it is one's consciousness that determines their spiritual destination.

Guru is Srila Prabhupada and Krishna himself. And if we have the opportunity and the chance to dedicate ourselves to Krishna, our life, to Prabhupada's mission, to our Guru Maharaj's mission, then there is a very high degree of chance that every activity that we do, we will be able to surrender the result to Krishna. So the situation becomes more favorable when we are a full time devotee, but ultimately it is the consciousness of that individual which determines whether it goes back to Godhead or not. So this is the fundamental point which we have to always keep in mind. Otherwise, being a full time missionary, yes, is more definitely far more conducive to be able to practice Krishna consciousness and to be able to come to the point of remembering Krishna all the time, which will help us to remember Krishna even at the time of death and take us back to Godhead. Okay.

## AAQ: Does Modern Science Believe in Reincarnation  |  His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### science, reincarnation, belief
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XmSILnukHO8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It's not that if you are a believer in science, you cannot be a believer in God. It's not like that. Because both are different branches of knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of whether modern science accepts reincarnation, stating that opinions vary among scientists and that belief in science does not preclude belief in God.

I believe in reincarnation. Does modern science accept it or not? Modern science, well, it ranges from 0 to 100. There are people in science who don't believe in reincarnation. There are people who believe somewhere in between. There are people who believe totally in reincarnation. So we have all kinds of people. It's not that if you are a believer in science, you cannot be a believer in God. It's not like that. Because both are different branches of knowledge. It's like asking, does someone who believes in physics really believe in mathematics? Or someone who believes in mathematics really believe in biology? Both are different branches of knowledge. So you cannot compare both of them. But yes, there are people who believe in God. There are people who believe in science who don't believe in reincarnation, don't believe in the existence of soul, etc. And there are many scientists, Nobel laureates, those who have achieved a lot in the field of chemistry, science, physics, etc. There are many people like that who believe that there must be a higher being behind this creation. There must be a higher intelligence behind this creation.

---
### material energy, spiritual energy, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XmSILnukHO8?t=112
**Verse References:** BG 7.4, BG 7.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And all the living entities are trying to control the material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the Vaishnava understanding of material and spiritual energy based on the Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing the living entity's attempt to control material energy, and contrasting this with the scientific view.

So there are all kinds of people. So we go by, see, the point is that science deals with matter, material energy. According to the understanding of the Bhagavad Gita, apareyam itas tv anyam prakritim viddhi me param, Krishna says, first of all, there is, first of all, Krishna says, there is one energy of his, which is called the differentiated energy or the interior energy. And this interior energy of Krishna, it consists of earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intelligence and falsity. These eight elements constitute the interior energy of Krishna. Apareyam itas tv anyam prakritim viddhi me param jiva-bhutam maha-baho yayedam dharyate jagat. And then Krishna says, the entire material energy that we see comprises different combinations of these eight elements. Whether you look at a human body, animal body, tree body, earth, planets, different planets, all of them comprise these fundamental eight elements. In varying proportions, varying forms, etc. But Krishna says, beyond this, there is a higher energy, superior energy of mine, which is called the living entity, jiva-bhutam yayedam dharyate jagat. And all the living entities are trying to control the material energy. You see how Krishna has beautifully put it. If you analyze this material, entire material manifestation also, you have matter, which is what scientists are all studying. Then there is the scientist who studies that matter, who wants to control all that matter by studying it. There are two distinct things, isn't it? One is matter itself. Then there is the person who is trying to harness material energy, control it, use it according to his purposes.

---
### artificial intelligence, consciousness, materialism

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the Vaishnava view of a separate living force with the scientific view that consciousness arises from material interactions, using the example of AI development to illustrate the point.

So Krishna brings this distinction out. And he says, both of them are different energies. Even the living entity is also energy of Krishna. Many scientists, of course, they do not accept that living force is a separate energy. They say that no, living force is also part of the material energy. Some point of time, when the chemicals mix together, consciousness is produced. That's all. And so that's why they are saying very quickly, we will produce sentient AI. And once we are there, we have produced life. Okay. Sentient AI already to some extent, they have already done. If you look at, if you go to Google, there is an option, Google Lens. You take a picture of something and put it into Google. It will search and bring out all the information related to it. Just the other day, somebody was asking us, if you see next to our compound wall, there are a lot of trees. There are those big, big fruits coming from the tree hanging down. So somebody asked, there must be some use for this fruit. So much of it is hanging down. Why are you not using it? So I immediately pulled out my mobile, took a picture and searched and found out that the fruit is poisonous. So you cannot use it. So I said, see, it is here. So you see, vision, form, identifying form. Our sight is what? Our power of sight is to identify forms and to be able to discriminate and determine. Okay. This form belongs to that of a speaker. This is the form of a fan. This is the form of a TV. We see the form and identify what the object is, what its functions are. Right. Similarly, AI is doing that. You capture a photograph. It will give you all information, what it is all about. So power of sight is already there. Right. And power of sound is already there. Now, latest thing is you hum a tune. Some song is running in your mind. You are remembering the tune, but you have forgotten the song. You hum a tune into your mobile and search, it will search out that song and tell you. I don't know whether you have used that feature. So even sound, it is able to, and also we are able to see that Siri and in Google also, there is one lady. You speak to her and tell, call this person. It will immediately, she will immediately call. You ask for directions, she will show from where to where, how to go. So sound also is recognizing. And you can dictate. Now it has become part of all softwares. Word, MS word you open, olden days we had to sit and type. Now we are all from that dinosaur age. So we are, you know, automatically tuned to open a word file and start typing. But now you don't have to do that. Open a word file and start dictating. Whichever language you want. You dictate in Tamil, it will type in Tamil. You dictate in Hindi, it will type in Hindi. You dictate in English, it will type in English. So sound also it has got recognized. So AI, now what is pending in AI?

### artificial intelligence, intelligence, creation

**Summary:** The speaker argues that AI is an imitation of human intelligence, requiring an intelligent creator, and therefore cannot be used to argue that life arose spontaneously from chemicals.

So you see people get carried away by this kind of imitations after all ultimately what is the experience it is imitating mimicking human intelligence that's all know so we are using our human intelligence and mimicking imitating original intelligence so AI is created you did not come up with that that is no off so we all gotta communicate Napoleon about it that is looping in the air modifications is created by us it is created by an intelligent human being so to create an imitation you need a living person so how can you say that the original has come into existence on its own that it has come about just by a combination of chemicals so ai also you you know allow chemicals to combine and form ai why it is not happening it will not happen so you see anything that we have to create requires intelligence creating some meaningful object cannot happen without intelligence some by chance some chemicals came together and created a beautiful object not possible so meaningful objects can be created only when you have a person behind it and that person cannot be created by a combination of chemicals so the person is beyond the ordinary or intelligent physical form called material energy. ### material energy, spiritual energy, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XmSILnukHO8?t=667
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is a distinct difference between a living person and dead matter."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the distinction between material and spiritual energy, emphasizing that spiritual energy is superior due to its controlling tendency and consciousness, which material energy lacks.

One is material energy, another is spiritual energy. Spiritual energy is superior because it is trying to control and manipulate the inferior material energy. Why is one called the inferior and the other called superior? Because the superior energy has got the controlling tendency and manipulating tendency, whereas the other energy does not have. The spiritual energy, the superior energy has consciousness, therefore it is superior. Material energy cannot possess consciousness at any point of time, therefore it is inferior. So this is how we have to see distinctly, you know, any person with common sense can make out this distinction. There is a distinct difference between a living person and dead matter. And that is why very simple example, as long as we are alive, we look at each other and call each other by names. We go to a hospital, they will ask for the medical record and they will say so and so person is getting treated here and all that. And suppose a person goes to the hospital and he dies in the hospital. What happens? The doctor will come and tell, please take the dead body and go. They will not tell the name of the person because no more, that person is no more, that person is gone. Only body has been left behind. Very simple, no, what is the difference between a living person and a dead body? It is the person who is there within the body. So it's very common sense. It doesn't require much, very great intelligence to understand this.

---
### illusion, materialism, science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XmSILnukHO8?t=770
**Verse References:** BG 7.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Their knowledge has been snatched away by the glamour, the glitter of material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes scientists who, despite their intelligence, fail to recognize the simple truth of the soul's existence due to being deluded by the allure of material energy and scientific progress.

But unfortunately, scientists, although they are apparently very intelligent, they are not able to see even this simple truth. That is why Krishna refers to them in the Bhagavad Gita as mayayapahrta-jnana. Their knowledge has been snatched away by the glamour, the glitter of material energy. They will invent one AI and they will say, we are almost there. We are going to create sentient air. We have, we have figured out how to create light. And they go on like this, like this. They will, you know, they will die. Their sons will come. They will also die. Their grandchildren will come. They will also die. Each of them will keep telling the people of their generation. We are almost there. We have figured it out. Don't worry. And life will go on. Death will go on. Business of science will also go on like this. So we should not get carried away by the glitter of the material energy. That's the whole thing. If you are carried away by the glitter of material energy, how much science and technology has had to do with it, how much science has advanced, what all discoveries have happened. So when you look at all those things, yes, we will be able to figure out what are the chemicals bringing about consciousness and life. This is how maya covers the intelligence of the living entity. This is called the avarana atmika shakti of maya. Material energy, although it is inferior, it has the ability to blind us with her glitter, promising something in the future. In the future, we are going to find out. In the future, we are going to be able to create, recreate life. So these kinds of false assurances are given to us by maya which covers our intelligence and we are unable to see the simple stark reality.

---
### reincarnation, belief, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XmSILnukHO8?t=890
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Amongst the scientists, those who are mayayapahrta-jnana, their intelligence has been stolen away by maya, will not be able to see how reincarnation is happening."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that belief in reincarnation depends on whether one's intelligence is covered by illusion, and notes the diversity of beliefs among scientists, some of whom acknowledge a higher intelligence.

This is what the Vedic scriptures are pointing out. So it doesn't matter. Amongst the scientists, those who are mayayapahrta-jnana, their intelligence has been stolen away by maya, will not be able to see how reincarnation is happening. Whereas those who are not completely bewildered by that illusory energy will be able to see the truth. That's why among scientists, you will see some of them are definitely, they believe in God. There are many others who don't believe in God. So many scientists will say, religion is all not required. We should remove religion. We should remove religion. We should remove religion. We should remove religion. We should remove religion. We should remove religion. And there in his row, one scientist will be releasing one satellite and before that he'll be going and doing one Ganesha puja. So you have both varieties. Because ultimately human beings, human beings, some people whose intelligence is not so much covered will be able to see that beyond our efforts and beyond our intelligence, there is a higher intelligence and we are dependent on that. So that's how things are.

## AAQ: How do we not Overthink, Over Analyze | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### faith, knowledge, modern skepticism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kmO-HeLRBHw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "To acquire spiritual knowledge, you need faith."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the question of how to avoid overthinking and overanalyzing, emphasizing the importance of faith in acquiring knowledge, particularly spiritual knowledge, contrasting it with the modern skeptical view.

Interesting question. How do we not overthink or overanalyze? How to avoid overthinking and overanalyzing? Well, if we look at the Vedic scriptures, if we look at the Bhagavad Gita, faith is given a very important role to play in the acquisition of knowledge. Now, of course, in the modern day, faith is treated as a very contemptuous thing. All religions are all fakes. Leave it aside. Science is real knowledge. That's the way it is presented.

---
### faith, science, spiritual knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kmO-HeLRBHw?t=56
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Faith is common for any acquisition of knowledge, whether biology, botany, chemistry, physics, mathematics, any branch of knowledge if you want to acquire, faith is common."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that faith is essential not only for spiritual knowledge but also for scientific knowledge, using the example of accepting scientific facts from textbooks without personal verification.

It is true that to acquire spiritual knowledge, you need faith. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita that ashraddha dana, those who are unfaithful, those who do not have faith, they will have neither peace in this life nor in the next one. So to be able to acquire spiritual knowledge, faith is very important. But then to acquire science also faith is required. All of us, we learn science. How? By going to the school and college, sitting in a classroom and studying the textbook. Whatever the textbook is teaching, we accept it as truth. We say to water, consciousness, there are two molecules of water. All of us have done it. How many of you have actually taken out water and analyzed how many atoms of hydrogen, how many atoms of oxygen are there? How many of you have done it? None of us have done it. True, no? But if anybody asks any one of us, what is the composition of water? What is the chemical composition of water? H2O. Immediately answer will come. Where did you get this H2O from? What is hydrogen in the first place? What is oxygen? Oxygen, can you separate it out and show it to me? If you can you show it to me, where is oxygen? Show me. Can you show me? Not possible. Can you show me hydrogen? Not possible. Can you show me God? Not possible. So can you show me God? I cannot show you God because you cannot see. So God does not exist. You cannot show me hydrogen, how should I accept that hydrogen exists? You are telling periodic table you are showing and all those things. Very fine. All right. That table anybody can make. Even a child can sit and make. So just because you made it and you put it in the textbook, I have to accept. So when you teach from a book, we have to accept it blindly. I have faith. This is a science book. Accept it. That's all. When it comes to same Bhagavad Gita, that is also a book. No, no, no, it is faith. Faith is common for any acquisition of knowledge, whether biology, botany, chemistry, physics, mathematics, any branch of knowledge if you want to acquire, faith is common.

---
### faith, authorized source, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kmO-HeLRBHw?t=194
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Faith in an authorized source of knowledge is essential for acquiring knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that it's not blind faith, but faith in an authorized source of knowledge, drawing a parallel between trusting science books from authorized institutions and trusting spiritual texts.

But not blind faith. Faith in an authorized source of that knowledge. Why we have faith in the science books? Because the science books are being taught in an authorized college, by an authorized professor. We are not going to some sabjeevala and learning what is the components of water, what is the chemical composition of water. We are not going to some street vendor who is illiterate and learning from him, right? We are going to an authorized college and authorized professor and authorized textbooks we are studying and we are learning from that. So that faith in an authorized source of knowledge is essential for acquiring knowledge. So that faith in an authorized source of knowledge is essential for acquiring knowledge. Any branch of knowledge without that faith, fundamental faith, nobody can acquire any knowledge. This is the reality.

---
### spiritual knowledge, authorized scriptures, validation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kmO-HeLRBHw?t=260
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Same principle works even for spiritual knowledge also."
**Summary:** The speaker extends the analogy, stating that for spiritual knowledge, authorized scriptures like Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam are the textbooks, and applying the principles in life is the experiment to validate the knowledge.

So, similarly, in for acquiring spiritual knowledge also, you have to have faith in the authorized source of that spiritual knowledge. So, just like we have textbooks which are prescribed by the university which are called authorized sources of knowledge, material knowledge, but similarly, for acquiring spiritual knowledge, the authorized books are Bhagavad Gita, Srimad Bhagavatam and other Vedic scriptures. So if we acquire knowledge from them, we will be able to, we will, if we have faith in those authorized books of spiritual knowledge, we will be able to acquire spiritual knowledge. And just like in the science books, first you have faith in the college and the teacher and the textbook, go and sit in the classroom, hear the theory, then you go to the lab and conduct the experiment. You will be able to see for yourself how the theory is correct, yes or no? But for you to conduct that experiment also, first you should have faith that yes, college is authorized, textbook is authorized, this knowledge being taught to me is authorized. If you have no faith, hey, this is all bogus, yeah, you will never acquire any knowledge. So with that faith, you first go and learn the theory. Then you go to the lab and do the practical experiments. Then whatever you learned in theory gets validated, our faith in that source of knowledge becomes stronger. This is the phenomenon which is working in science also. Same thing, same principle works even for spiritual knowledge also.

Analyzing what it simply means is we are not having sufficient faith in the source of knowledge, so if you have faith in that source of knowledge if you have done a little bit of analysis and determined for yourself that yes i accept that this is a bona fide source of knowledge then you don't after that analyze whether this source what the source is saying is correct or not you accept that whatever is coming from the thoughts is correct so then we have we become free from overthinking and over analyzing but if all our life we keep questioning the source then we will never be able to acquire knowledge same thing you go to college first you determine college has certain registration accreditation it has got a recognition you analyze all those things and then you look at who are the professors what are their qualifications and how many years they have been teaching so you analyze all this and come to a conclusion yes this college is authorized after that you get into the college admit yourself in the college then whatever they teach you have to accept this accept it as school right now after admission three years you are in the college still you are researching is this college authorized or not and then why are in the college via this is called over analysis and working so we have to learn to place faith after an initial assessment of the of the authenticity of the source of our knowledge once we have determined that this source of knowledge is authentic whatever comes from there we have to accept it as then we will become free from overthinking and over analysis so the root cause of overthinking and over analysis is what lack of sufficient faith.
