## Mahabharata | Part 50 | Duryodhana seeks Krishna's & Balarama's support | HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### diplomacy, war, justice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=104
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Dhritarashtra wanted to send a message which would not be hostile, which would not incite war, but at the same time somehow broker peace."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the previous episode and sets the stage for the current one, focusing on Dhritarashtra's desire to avoid war while Duryodhana remains unwilling to return Indraprastha.

In the last episode of the Mahabharata, we have seen how Drupada Maharaj sent his priest as a messenger to Hastinapura to convey their intent and requesting the Kauravas to return Indraprastha to the Pandavas, while also assessing what was the mood of the Kauravas and what all preparations they were undertaking. And we also saw how the Kauravas were completely against returning the kingdom to Indraprastha. Duryodhana, being the envious, greedy person that he was, he had no intention of returning the kingdom. Shakuni had made his own calculations, some convoluted calculations he had done to convince others that the Pandavas had not yet completed their term of exile, living incognito, before they were discovered. And on that basis, they should be asked to go back for another 12 years of forest exile. Although in reality, just as Bhishma Dev had done his calculations, the Pandavas had completed their exile period successfully. Then after this, Dhritarashtra thought of sending Sanjaya, who was his charioteer and his personal secretary, to the Pandavas with a message which he, after hearing out Duryodhana and understanding his intentions, that he did not want to return the kingdom of Indraprastha. So Dhritarashtra wanted to send a message which would not be hostile, which would not incite war, but at the same time somehow broker peace. That was Dhritarashtra's thinking. So what did Sanjaya convey to the Pandavas? What was the message he took to them? What was their reaction? Is what we will see today.

---
### strategy, Krishna's influence, Duryodhana's ambition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So he thought, let me quickly go to Dwaraka and seek the support of the Yadava army."
**Summary:** The segment describes Duryodhana's strategy to secure the Yadava army's support, recognizing their invincibility and Krishna's influence, leading him to personally visit Dwaraka.

In the meantime, what had happened was, when after the marriage of Uttara and Abhimanyu, when all the kings had got together and they were strategizing what should be the next steps to be taken, and they finally decided to send Drupada's priest to Hastinapura to seek a return of the Pandavas' kingdom, Lord Krishna and Balarama had returned to Dwaraka, giving confidence to the Pandavas that whenever they would be needed, they would be there. This news that Krishna had returned to Dwaraka reached Hastinapura. Now Duryodhana had already started intimating his allies, gathering different armies, and he was building up his army very well aware that his refusal to return Indraprastha to the Pandavas, which was rightfully theirs, would inevitably result in war. Now the Yadava Sainya, which was called Narayani Sainya, it was known to be an army which was invincible. The Narayani Sena of Krishna had defeated Jarasandha innumerable number of times. They had defeated Shalva, and many such battles had been very easily won by the Narayani Sena. And all over the world, everybody knew that the Yadava army was practically invincible. Now it was Duryodhana also knew that Krishna always was favorable towards the Pandavas. So he thought, let me quickly go to Dwaraka and seek the support of the Yadava army. While to all other allies, Duryodhana had sent his different emissaries to seek the support of the different kings. When it came to the Yadava army, because it was so important, Duryodhana personally went to Dwaraka. Duryodhana came to Dwaraka and he informed the people there that he had come to meet Lord Krishna because he knew, although Ugrasena was the king of the Yadavas, still Krishna's word was always considered final in the Yadava assembly. So he thought it is wise to meet Krishna and if Krishna, if he seeks Krishna's support and Krishna agrees, then all the rest of the entire Yadava clan would definitely support him. So with this intention, Duryodhana had come to Dwaraka.

### devotion, relationships, divine arrangement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna had also come seeking the support of Krishna practically at the same time."
**Summary:** This segment describes Satyaki joining the Pandavas, Duryodhana's arrival at Krishna's chambers, and Arjuna's simultaneous arrival, highlighting the close relationship between the Kauravas and Yadavas.

In the meantime, what had happened is, before Duryodhana came to Dwaraka, Satyaki, one of the commander-in-chiefs of the Yadava army, he was a disciple of Arjuna. He had learnt the art of archery from Arjuna himself and he was always very much in favour of the Pandavas because of their righteousness. Satyaki, without waiting for an invitation from the Pandavas, took one Akshauhini, one division of army and went to Virata, the kingdom of Virata and he joined the Pandavas. Now the rest of the Yadava clan was still in Dwaraka by the time Duryodhana had reached Dwaraka and now when Duryodhana came and sought audience with Krishna, he was told that Krishna was taking rest within his chambers and so he was escorted, Duryodhana was escorted towards Krishna's chambers. Because Duryodhana, the entire Kaurava dynasty were closely related with the Yadava dynasty because Kunti Devi was from the Yadava dynasty and therefore she was the aunt of Lord Krishna and that way the relationship was quite thick between the Yadava household and the Kaurava household. So therefore, Duryodhana had access to Krishna's private chambers. When Duryodhana arrived, it so happened that Krishna was actually resting. He was sleeping in his inner chambers and because he was a close family member, he was escorted to Krishna's bedroom. As Duryodhana was entering Krishna's bedroom, he saw there was one more visitor who had arrived at the same time. It was none other than, Arjuna. Arjuna had also come seeking the support of Krishna practically at the same time. Now Duryodhana and Arjuna saw each other, they greeted each other and Duryodhana entered first into Krishna's chamber because he had come first and also he was the elder one, Arjuna was younger. Giving due respect according to his age, Arjuna followed Duryodhana into the chamber.

---
### Krishna's diplomacy, devotion, Vedic principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But when I opened my eyes, the first person I saw was Arjuna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the scene of Duryodhana and Arjuna waiting for Krishna, Krishna's awakening, and his diplomatic offer to divide his support between them, giving Arjuna the first choice.

As soon as Duryodhana entered, he saw Krishna lying on the bedstead and next to Krishna near his head was a small chair. Duryodhana went and sat there at Krishna's head waiting for him to wake up. Arjuna in the meantime, he also entered and he saw Krishna is sleeping and he went and stood very respectfully at the lotus feet of Krishna. Arjuna, he is a great devotee of Lord Krishna, and for him his heart is filled with love for Krishna. Yes, he understands the great position of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So he felt it appropriate to stand at the lotus feet of Krishna. And both Duryodhana and Arjuna were silently waiting, not wanting to disturb the sleep of Krishna. After a little while, Krishna slowly opened his eyes. And when Krishna opened his eyes, he saw Arjuna standing at his feet. And then slowly Krishna raised himself up and he said, Oh Arjuna, you have come here. And then when Krishna got up and sat up on his bed, he saw Duryodhana is sitting next to him. And he said, Oh Duryodhana, you have also come. Then Krishna greeted both of them warmly and he asked them, Why have you both come at the same time together? At the same time to meet me? Then Duryodhana replied to Krishna and he said, Oh my dear Krishna, we both have come here seeking your support and the support of your Yadava army for any eventuality of any war that may take place between us. And you are related to the Kaurava dynasty. So you are equally related to me as well as to the Pandavas. Therefore, you should be equal to both of us. And since I have come here first, therefore I have the right to first ask your support. Lord Krishna replied to Duryodhana, My dear Duryodhana, I agree what you have said is right. You came here first. But when I opened my eyes, the first person I saw was Arjuna. Not only that, Arjuna is younger to you by age. And according to the Vedic scriptures, according to Dharma, the younger person is always given the first opportunity. And so therefore, I give Arjuna the opportunity to seek my support.

---
### devotion, Krishna's supremacy, Arjuna's choice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=781
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna, doesn't matter that you don't lift arms, you don't do any activity, you just be by my side. That's enough for me."
**Summary:** Arjuna chooses Krishna over the Yadava army, expressing his devotion and prioritizing Krishna's presence above all else, much to Duryodhana's delight.

But at the same time, what you have said is right, Duryodhana, that I am related equally to both of you. This is a family quarrel. And so, what I will do is, on one side, I present myself unarmed. I will not take up arms in the battle. I will not fight in the battle. But I will be there giving good advice and support. Moral support and advice will be there from my side. On the other hand, my entire Narayani Sena, the Yadavasanya will be available. So between the two of you, you can choose one of these two. And Arjuna, I give you the first opportunity please choose whom you want. Arjuna, without batting an eyelid, without hesitation, immediately said, Krishna, doesn't matter that you don't lift arms, you don't do any activity, you just be by my side. That's enough for me. Krishna said, okay, as you wish. Then he turned to Duryodhana and he said, Arjuna has chosen me. So I am afraid that you have to be satisfied with the Narayana. So I am afraid that you have to be satisfied with the Narayani Sena. Duryodhana, internally, was delighted. He had thought that oh, Arjuna has got the first right, he will choose the Narayani Sena and I will be left with Krishna who is anyway not going to lift weapons. What is the point of having him then anyway on our side? Duryodhana was elated that Arjuna had made the foolish decision. And he thought, good, Arjuna has somehow, by some fluke, Arjuna has chosen wrongly.

### Krishna's grace, devotion, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=922
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are the goal of my life. So I don't want anything else. Everything else is secondary. If you are there with me, for me, everything else is achieved."
**Summary:** Arjuna explains his choice, emphasizing Krishna's supreme position and his desire for Krishna's presence above all else, leading Krishna to agree to be Arjuna's charioteer.

So Duryodhana, but externally, he was a greatly diplomatic person. He maintained a very calm exterior and neutral countenance he maintained. And he said, I think I don't have a choice now. Arjuna has already chosen, you have given him the first right. So I have to be satisfied with your Narayani Sena. Okay, all right. I'll be satisfied with that. Okay, thank you both of you. I'm leaving. And he said, he got up and he walked out. Now when Duryodhana walked out, Krishna looked at Arjuna and he said, Arjuna, I said, I will be, I will not take up arms. Then why did you choose me? You should have taken the army. The entire Yadava army was at your disposal. Arjuna immediately said, Oh Krishna, you are the supreme personality of Godhead. Wherever you are there, Lakshmi Devi is also there. That means all opulence is also there. All victory is also there. I am not bothered about winning or losing this battle. I want you. You are the person whom I want. You are the goal of my life. So I don't want anything else. Everything else is secondary. If you are there with me, for me, everything else is achieved. So Krishna was overwhelmed by this love from Arjuna. And he said, and he told Arjuna, Arjuna, I am, I am indebted to you for the love you have shown me. So please tell me how I can help you in this battle. Then Arjuna told Krishna that Krishna has a great desire. I want you to be my charioteer in this battle. Krishna readily agreed. He said, is that all? Definitely I'll be your charioteer. You can see Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. Krishna's lotus feet are worshipped even by Lord Shiva and Lord Brahma. All the devatas are waiting for an opportunity to have darshan of the lotus feet of Krishna. Krishna is such an exalted personality. He is the supreme personality of Godhead. There is nobody equal to him, nobody above him. Such is the greatness of Krishna. He is bhoktaram yajna tapasam sarva loka mahishuram. All the universes, all the planetary systems are emanating from Krishna. Krishna is the supreme creator. And therefore everything belongs to him. And all the ajnas, all the sacrifices, all the Vedas are meant for pleasing Krishna. And that Krishna is agreeing to become the charioteer of Arjuna, his devotee. These are the loving dealings between the Lord, between Lord Krishna, the supreme personality of Godhead, and his devotees. Although Krishna is the supreme person, he is the master of everyone. Still Krishna loves to become subordinate to the commands of his devotee.

---
### Krishna's expansions, Ananda, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1062
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By nature, the supreme lord being a person, he desires to enjoy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Duryodhana's next move to seek Balarama's support and then delves into the philosophical concept of Krishna's expansions for the purpose of enjoyment and relationship.

In the meantime, Duryodhana had still not given up. His mind was constantly working. He thought, okay, let's refuse to partake in the battle. But what about Balarama? He is the next most powerful personality. While Arjuna and Krishna were discussing like this, Duryodhana had left Krishna's room and he had gone to approach Balarama. The added advantage for Duryodhana with Balarama was Balarama was his guru as far as learning the art of fighting with the gatha, with the mace was concerned. So Duryodhana already had a good relationship with Balarama. And very quickly he approached Balarama before Arjuna could approach. And Duryodhana approached Balarama and he told him that like this, I have come here seeking your support and I approached Lord Krishna also. But Krishna has agreed to become, he told that he would decide, he would divide the Narayani Sena and himself. One side he will be there without arms and the other side will be his army. And Arjuna chose Krishna. Now I have come to you seeking your support. Now Duryodhana did not know the actual position of Balarama. Actually Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. Now it is stated in the Vedas that Anandamayo Abhyasat, by nature, the supreme lord being a person, he desires to enjoy. And when he desires to enjoy, what does he do? Just like in this material world, whenever we want to enjoy, what do we do? We enter into relationships with other individuals. We enjoy in relationship with our families and with our friends and relatives etc. We do not enjoy if we are all alone. If we have got something nice to eat, then we want to call a few friends and we want to share it with them. If we want to watch a movie, we want to go with our family. If we want to go on a vacation, we want to take our wives and children along with us. This is the nature of a person, of an individual person. This nature of human beings is actually a result of being of the individual nature. Being a part and parcel of the supreme lord. Because the supreme lord has a propensity to enjoy in relationship with others, we being emanations, we being part and parcel of that supreme lord, we also have, we also possess similar propensity to enjoy with other individuals.

---
### Balarama's devotion, Krishna's expansions, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Balarama is very very possessive of Krishna and he will not think of doing anything other than what Krishna likes."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Krishna's expansions, focusing on Balarama's role as Krishna's first expansion and his unwavering devotion, leading Balarama to refuse to oppose Krishna in the upcoming war.

Now, Krishna when he desires to enjoy, he expands himself. He is not dependent upon individuals other than himself to enjoy. He expands himself into other personalities. He expands himself into first as Balarama. And that Balarama further expands into the Chaturvyuha expansion of Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha. And from that Sankarsana again expands Narayana. From that Narayana, the second quadruple expansion of Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha come out. And from that second Sankarsana come the Vishnu avataras within this material world. So like this, the lord expands himself into innumerable forms of the Vishnu tattva as well as his incarnation. And the lord also expands himself into his devotees. And then the lord expands himself into all the living entities that we all are. And so like this, the lord expands himself into unlimited forms to engage in wonderful loving relationships with different individuals. Now Balarama is the first expansion of Krishna. And he is imbued with love for Krishna. When the lord descended as Ramachandra, the same Balarama descended as Lakshmana. And in the past times of Lord Ramachandra, Lord Ramachandra had to undergo so much difficulty. And Lakshmana being a younger brother, many times he had great desire to partake, to express his anguish whenever he saw Lord Ramachandra suffering. But he could not express because he was a younger brother and he was inhibited by that subordinate position. So when the Supreme Lord Krishna personally descended in this world, Balarama came as his elder brother so that he could express his anguish whenever Krishna would feel any sort of distress. And also Balarama could play a protective role and prevent anybody from causing any harm or distress to Krishna. So Balarama is very very possessive of Krishna and he will not think of doing anything other than what Krishna likes. The moment when Balarama heard that Krishna has already given himself to the Pandavas, Balarama told Duryodhana, I cannot even dream of opposing Krishna in any manner. And because this is a family quarrel, I will not participate in this. Let Krishna give support to the Pandavas. I am giving up my Kshatriya Dharma. I am retiring from this Kshatriya duties of fighting and engaging in battle and all these kind of things. I am going on Teertha Yatra. Balarama decided to go on pilgrimage.

### diplomacy, peace, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1434
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Return Indraprastha to us. Matter will be settled."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the episode of Duryodhana seeking support and transitions to Sanjaya's arrival at the Pandavas' camp with Dhritarashtra's message of peace.

And so Duryodhana thought, okay, anyway Balarama did not come to my side, but he is not going to fight on the side of the Pandavas also. So there is no loss. Then he said, okay, fine, alright. If you don't want to fight, no problem. And Duryodhana went back to Hastinapura. And in this manner, Krishna became the charioteer of Arjuna. And now Arjuna took Krishna with him and went back to the kingdom of Virata for further consultation and seeking Krishna's advice on what they should do next. After Krishna and Arjuna had reached the kingdom of Virata, they came to know that Sanjaya has come there, bringing the message of Dhritarashtra. The Pandavas, they all assembled and they called forth Sanjaya. Sanjaya came there. After receiving all the welcoming hospitality, Sanjaya came forward into that assembly and Yudhishthira Maharaj received him very warmly because the Pandavas knew Sanjaya for a long time. And Sanjaya had also been always very favorable towards the Pandavas. Although he was the secretary of Dhritarashtra, he knew that the Pandavas were righteous and he was always favorable to them. This Sanjaya came before the Pandavas and Yudhishthira Maharaj asked about the welfare of all the senior people in the Kaurava household like Bhishma Dev, Dronacharya, Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, etc. And Sanjaya also told Yudhishthira and the Pandavas that they were all doing well and that he has brought a message from Dhritarashtra. Yudhishthira Maharaj said, please go ahead, please let us know what message you are bringing from Dhritarashtra. Then Sanjaya told Yudhishthira that Dhritarashtra, King Dhritarashtra wants peace. He does not want any kind of fighting, infighting. And he has said that he is ready to consider all options to avoid any kind of war or any kind of skirmish, and let us peacefully settle the matter. And he has also said that there is no point in fighting, infighting between the family members. If the family members kill each other, then what are we achieving? We are not achieving anything. It is not good for either party. So let us avoid fighting. This is the message.

---
### justice, Dharma, war
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1591
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You please first consider what is the duty of a Kshatriya."
**Summary:** Yudhishthira responds to Sanjaya's message, demanding the return of Indraprastha and asserting the Pandavas' readiness for war if justice is not served, leading to a debate on Dharma and the duty of a Kshatriya.

So Yudhishthira replied to Sanjaya, See, if you want peace, the solution is very simple. Return Indraprastha to us. Matter will be settled. I assure you that we will forget all animosity, whatever the Kauravas, they have insulted us, whatever humiliation they have done towards us, how they cheated us in the game of dice, we will forget all that and we will live as friends. I assure you this. On the other hand, if you do not hand over Indraprastha to us, then we are not sitting here idly. Arjuna is having the Gandiva in his hand and Bhima is having his Gada in his hand and you will have to reply to these two people and no force in this world can withstand the force of these two weapons of my brothers. When Sanjaya heard this, he was surprised. He said, Yudhishthira, how is it that you are so much inclined to war? You should be thinking of how to get peace. A righteous person is never inclined towards war and to quarrel and such things. He should always think of how to maintain peace and harmony in the society because wars always bring untold suffering to humanity. Everyone knows this. So how is it that you are behaving like this adharmically? When Sanjaya said this, Yudhishthira said that you please first consider what is the duty of a Kshatriya. Duty of a Kshatriya is to fight or not in the face of injustice. In the face of injustice, is a Kshatriya supposed to keep quiet? And what is happening with us here? Is it just or is it unjust? So on whose side is Dharma? You please consider this. And then Yudhishthira Maharaj turned to Krishna and he said, Lord Krishna, you decide whether what I am saying is right or wrong. You please tell us whether I am wrong in expressing that in case the kingdom is not given, there has to be war. Am I a war mongering person? Am I going against the principles of Dharma? Then Sanjaya also looked towards Krishna because Sanjaya also knew that Krishna is the Supreme Lord.

### justice, Dharma, Krishna's anger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1758
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now what right do you have to speak of Dharma?"
**Summary:** Krishna responds to Yudhishthira and Sanjaya, expressing his anger at the Kauravas' past injustices and firmly stating that the kingdom must be returned, or there will be war.

Krishna responded that Yudhishthira, whatever you have said is perfectly according to the codes of Dharma. You have always lived righteously and your demand for the return of your kingdom is also righteous. And Sanjaya suddenly, everybody could see Krishna's face becoming very grave and they could sense Krishna's rising anger. Krishna said, Sanjaya, how did the Kauravas get hold of Indraprastha? Do you remember? They cheated Yudhishthira Maharaj in that game of dice. Although Yudhishthira Maharaj was saying in that assembly that he did not wish to play that game of dice, still he was goaded into playing that game of dice. Although he was not so keen on playing that game of dice, unfairly Duryodhana made Shakuni his partner and he cheated Yudhishthira of his kingdom, of his brothers and his wife. And when Yudhishthira had lost his kingdom, at that point of time, what were you all doing? You were all sitting in the assembly keeping quiet? You did not stop? What was going on? The game of cheating dice? And then what happened? Draupadi was pulled into that assembly and she was attempted to be disrobed. When her honour was being violated, which person in that assembly stood up and spoke? Except for Vikarna. Did Bhishma oppose it? Did Drona oppose it? You were also there. Did you oppose it? So at that time, you did not think of Dharma. Now what right do you have to speak of Dharma? You have no right to talk about what is right and what is wrong. You have no right to talk about Dharma and Adharma. Forget it. You keep it aside. Whatever the kingdom has been taken by force by the Kauravas has to be returned. Otherwise there will be war and Duryodhana and all his brothers will bite the dust. So Krishna's anger was something which was unexpected. And everybody was taken aback, including Sanjaya. But Sanjaya also felt ashamed because whatever Krishna had said was true. In that assembly, Dushyasana was trying to disrobe Draupadi. When Karna was inciting Duryodhana to drag Draupadi into that assembly, none of them had opposed. Sanjaya had not opposed. Sanjaya had kept quiet. This was wrong on his part. Sanjaya accepted that. So he bowed his head and kept quiet. So finally, after little more discussion like this, Sanjaya asked the Pandavas, Please give me your message. What is the message I have to give to Dhritarashtra? Yudhishthir Maharaj told him, Our message is very simple. Please hand over Indraprastha, which is rightfully ours, back to us. We will forget all hostility and we will live as friends and brothers. On the other hand, if you wish to, if you do not give the kingdom back to us, then there has to be war. And even if you give five villages, one for each of five brothers, we are ready to even accept that because we are Kshatriyas. Our duty is to rule. As long as we have got a place to rule and some citizens to administer, then we have no issues. We are ready even for that. This is our message.

---
### peace, revenge, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1957
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am waiting to fulfill my vow."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the responses of Arjuna and Bhima to the message, highlighting Bhima's surprising plea for peace, followed by his reaffirmation of his vow for revenge, ending with a cliffhanger about the Kauravas' reaction.

And then Krishna spoke to Arjuna. Krishna asked Arjuna, Arjuna, what is your response? And Arjuna responded, He said, Whatever my brother Yudhishthir Maharaj has said is fully right and I stand fully with him. If the Kauravas are ready to give back Indraprastha, we will maintain peace. Otherwise, my Gandiva will speak. We will not speak anymore. Then Krishna looked at Bhima and he said, Bhima, what do you have to say? Now what was Bhima's response? Normally, we would expect Bhima to be the most angry of the lot and the person who was most thirsting for revenge. Surprisingly, Bhima spoke very peacefully and Bhima said, My dear Sanjaya, we want peace. We don't want any fight. Please tell Dhritarashtra that somehow please convince Duryodhana to give us at least five villages. We are happy with that. We don't want any fight. We don't want any battle. Why we should fight and kill each other unnecessarily? If we on one side and you Kauravas with Bhishma, Drona and all on the other side fight, what is the result which is going to happen? We will simply kill each other. I don't want to see such bloodshed. Let us avoid all this. Let us maintain peace. Krishna was surprised and Krishna started laughing. Krishna said, What is it that I am hearing Bhima? Looks like you have become afraid when the time for battle has come. What happened to all those words of revenge and all those things you were speaking? Then Bhima became very grave and he said, Krishna, it is not that I have become afraid. I was feeling compassionate towards the Kauravas because if there is battle, definitely they will be killed. And I thought, why they have to go through all this pain and Dhritarashtra and Gandhari will have to suffer seeing their sons killed before them. So I was being compassionate and thought, let us avoid it. But otherwise I am not afraid. I am waiting for battle. Let the Kaurava brothers come. I am waiting to fulfill my vow. I have vowed to kill Dushyasana and rip his heart out and drink his blood. I am waiting for the battle to happen between both of us. So Krishna smiled and said, and he said, This is the Bhima we know. Fine. So like this, everybody's message was given and Sanjaya started back to Hastinapura. And when he reached Hastinapura and how was the message from the Pandavas received? What was the reaction in the Kaurava? What was the reaction in the Kaurava palace? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna. Jagat Guru Shri La Pravupada Ki Jai Netai Gor Prem Anandae Hari Hari Bol

Mokam Karoti Vachalam Pangum Langajate Girim Yad Kripata Mahamande Shri Guram Dina Taranam. Hare Krishna and welcome back to this episode of the Mahabharata. In the previous episode, we have seen how Duryodhana and Arjuna approached Krishna for his support. Arjuna chose to have Krishna who would be beside the Pandavas. Arjuna was helping them, assisting them in the battle but unarmed and Duryodhana got the Yadava army and Balarama decided to go on pilgrimage not wishing to take part with either of the family members and then we also saw how Sanjaya brought Dhritarashtra's message to the Pandavas with actually no concrete proposal, just asking for peace and nothing more. They were not. He was not offering the kingdom back to the Pandavas. And the Pandavas gave their response and sent Sanjaya back.

### Deception, Hospitality, Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=153
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Duryodhana smiled slyly this was a cunning arrangement from him to make Shalya indebted so that he could seek his support so in a cunning way he wanted to divert Shalya to his camp"
**Summary:** The lecture narrates how King Shalya, intending to support the Pandavas, is tricked by Duryodhana's elaborate hospitality into fighting for the Kauravas, highlighting Duryodhana's cunning and Shalya's commitment to his word.

Now how was Sanjaya received in the Kaurava palace? That is what we will see today. Before that, when the message was reaching all the kings all over the world about the impending battle between Pandavas and Kauravas, he was not offering the kingdom back to the Pandavas. He was not offering the kingdom back to the Pandavas. country in the kingdom of Madhra, Madhra Desha, there was the king who was known as Shalya. He was the son of Somadatta and his brother was Burishtaba. His sister was Madhri and Madhri as we know was married to Pandu. When the message of this impending fight between the Pandavas and Kauravas reached Madhra Desha, Shalya wanted to support the Pandavas naturally because they were his sister's sons and so he took his army and set out to the kingdom of Virata. Now when Shalya was taking his army and proceeding towards the kingdom of Virata, he found that all along the way there had been a battle between the Pandavas and the Kauravas. The Kauravas were many nice encampments created for him and his soldiers to rest during the nights they would travel during the day and during the night they would set camp somewhere along the way and wherever they were supposed to put camp already tents were arranged various arrangements were made for their food and for their secure resting during the night and water good drinking water was made available clean water for their clothing for their bathing and for washing their clothes all these arrangements were made Shalya was very happy when he saw this he was pleasantly surprised so as they approached the kingdom of Virata at every stage he found wonderful arrangements were made Shalya was very happy he was thinking that the Pandavas knowing that I am coming to them for supporting them have made such wonderful arrangements Yudhishthira is really very grateful and kind and when they were close to the kingdom of Virata Shalya being so happy with all the arrangements because it took him so many days to come from Madhurdesha till here he could not he could not contain himself and he was saying where is Yudhishthira I want I really want to meet him I cannot wait till I reach the kingdom of Virata to meet him he has made such wonderful arrangements I am really obliged to him please he spoke to the servants who had been deployed there for taking care of the hospital of King Shalya and all his all his retinue he told them please go and call Yudhishthira I want to meet them these servants started looking at each other and with a puzzled look they stepped out of the camp and they started talking to each other Shalya is talking of Yudhishthira what is this all these arrangements have been made by Duryodhana so anyway let us go and talk to convey to Duryodhana they went and conveyed like this to Duryodhana like this Shalya is really obliged but he is thinking Yudhishthira has made all these arrangements and he wants to meet you to thank you and expresses indebtedness Duryodhana smiled slyly this was a cunning arrangement from him to make Shalya indebted so that he could seek his support so in a cunning way he wanted to divert Shalya to his camp and now Duryodhana now that he knew Shalya had taken all his hospitality and he was now indebted to him Duryodhana came before him and Shalya when he saw Duryodhana he was taken aback and he said Duryodhana what are you doing here Duryodhana said I am the one who have made all the arrangements for you and I have taken care of all your hospitality on route so that all your difficulties you don't have any difficulty in your travel see how cunning Duryodhana is now Shalya felt trapped to his nephews but he landed up with Duryodhana and he said okay Duryodhana I have taken all your hospitality so I am indebted to you now you please tell me whatever you want I am ready to give I cannot go back on my this is my word of honor because it is it is the it is for a respectable man if he has taken somebody's hospitality he has to fulfill his obligation towards the host Duryodhana then said I want you and your army to fight with me whenever we have to fight with the Pandavas now Shalya was in a fix he said Duryodhana what to do I understand what you have done now but now it's too late I have also without thinking without asking I enjoyed all the hospitality you have given me so I'm obliged to I'll keep my word of honor I am a man of word of my word of honor so I will since you have asked me I cannot refuse I will fight on your side.

### Dharma, Magnanimity, Leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=471
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira was more keen that the path of Dharma should be followed rather than his personal conveniences or his personal victory or gaining of the kingdom from the battle."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Duryodhana's small-mindedness with Yudhishthira's magnanimity, highlighting Yudhishthira's adherence to Dharma and his prioritization of righteousness over personal gain, which made him dear to great personalities and Lord Krishna.

I was extremely happy his ploy had worked he had cheated Shalya into joining him because Shalya was a powerful King and he had in effect what had happened Shalya had come with a certain army now that much Army was lesser in the pond Pandavas that much was more in the kauravas army so then Duryodhana said very fantastic now I request you please come with me to Hastinapura Shalya replied I will come to Hastinapura anyway I was going to kingdom of Virata. I want to meet my nephews. I will meet them. Then I will come to Hastinapura. And so then Duryodhana went back to Hastinapura. King Shalya proceeded to the kingdom of Virata. There he met Yudhishthira Maharaj and he explained to him what all had happened. And Yudhishthira Maharaj when he heard everything, he told King Shalya, my dear uncle, I feel that whatever you have done is the right thing. You should keep your word of honor. Although your support for us was invaluable, still your word of honor, the promise you have made is far more important. So you have done the right thing. Without any feeling of ill will, you may please proceed and be with Duryodhana. You can see how small-minded Duryodhana was and how magnanimous Yudhishthira was. Yudhishthira was a great man. Yudhishthira was more keen that the path of Dharma should be followed rather than his personal conveniences or his personal victory or gaining of the kingdom from the battle. That was less important for Yudhishthira Maharaj. And that is why Yudhishthira is so highly glorified and he was so dear to all the great personalities like Bhishma, Drona, etc. and even Lord Krishna himself.

---
### Dharma, Krishna Consciousness, Long-term consequences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=577
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we want to attain the favor of the Supreme Lord Krishna, and if we want everlasting fame, and we want that people should honor and respect one, then it is possible only if we follow the path of Dharma scrupulously."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of following Dharma and living a pious life to gain the favor of Lord Krishna and earn respect, contrasting it with Duryodhana's temporary success based on sin.

Actually, if we want to know the truth about Yudhishthira Maharaj, we need to know the truth about Yudhishthira Maharaj. If we want to attain the favor of the Supreme Lord Krishna, and if we want everlasting fame, and we want that people should honor and respect one, then it is possible only if we follow the path of Dharma scrupulously. If we follow a pious life, then automatically people will respect us and the Supreme Lord will also, he will become dear to us. On the other hand, if we take, if we accept a life which is based on sin, then like Duryodhana we may enjoy temporary opulence and power and all that, but we will never be dear to anyone not to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. This is something which we have to very clearly understand from the examples of Duryodhana and Yudhishthira Maharaj. We should not get swayed by instant success. We should look at what is the long-term repercussions of our actions and consequences of our actions.

---
### Strategy, Devotion, Kurukshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=654
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In such an event, you should do your best to demoralize Karna by uttering words which are unfavorable and which are discouraging to him. That will help Arjuna to win over Karna."
**Summary:** Yudhishthira requests King Shalya to demoralize Karna during the battle between Arjuna and Karna, and Shalya agrees, demonstrating Yudhishthira's love and concern for Arjuna's safety.

That is far more important than some petty immediate gains. Now, Dvideshwara Maharaj told the King Shalya, but there is still something that you can do for us. And King Shalya immediately became very eager and he said, please tell me, how can I help you in this battle? Well, if there is at all anything I can do, I am ready to do. Then Dvideshwara Maharaj told him, see, King Shalya, you are renowned for your skills in driving chariots. There is nobody who is equal to you in driving a chariot. So, during the battle of Kurukshetra, eventually it will come to a pass that when Arjuna and Karna have to face each other in battle, you will be asked to drive the chariot. In such an event, you should do your best to demoralize Karna by uttering words which are unfavorable and which are discouraging to him. That will help Arjuna to win over Karna. Of course, Arjuna did not need this help. Arjuna was supported by Lord Krishna, but still Yudhishthira, out of great love for his younger brother, out of... fear for his safety, Yudhishthira made this request. And King Shalya immediately assented and he said, oh, this is something which I can definitely do. I will keep this in mind and when the opportunity arises, I will definitely do my best. And having promised Yudhishthira Maharaj like this, King Shalya left and he proceeded towards Hastinapura with his army to join the... the bigger army, which Duryodhana had assembled.

---
### Diplomacy, Conflict, Advice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=767
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Sanjaya informed Dhritarashtra that Yudhishthira Maharaj is so righteous. He is even now saying a very simple thing. He is ready to accept Dhritarashtra."
**Summary:** Sanjaya returns to Hastinapura and informs Dhritarashtra of Yudhishthira's righteousness and willingness to accept peace, urging Dhritarashtra to reject Duryodhana and return the kingdom to the Pandavas, which is supported by Bhishma, Drona, and Vidura.

Now, when Sanjaya came back to Hastinapura, it was already evening, and he directly went and met Dhritarashtra. And Dhritarashtra asked Sanjaya what had transpired. Sanjaya informed Dhritarashtra that Yudhishthira Maharaj is so righteous. He is even now saying a very simple thing. He is ready to accept Dhritarashtra. He is ready to accept Dhritarashtra. He is ready to accept the kingdom back and forget all the animosity and hostility shown by Duryodhana. So, my dear King, you have always acted sinfully by supporting Duryodhana. Please, at least now, act in the right way. Reject Duryodhana. Hand over the kingdom back to the Pandavas and earn their goodwill. This is the only way we can live in peace. And then Dhritarashtra asked Sanjaya, okay, now tell me about the menace. What they have sent. And then Sanjaya told him, I am tired now. I will go and take rest. And morning I will come to the assembly. And in the assembly, in front of everyone, I will share the message. Next day morning, Sanjaya came into the assembly and he spoke all those words which Yudhishthira Maharaj had spoken as well as what Krishna had spoken and what Arjuna and Bhima had also spoken. When the Kaurava house, when the Kaurava council heard all this, Bhishma, Drona, Vidura, all these people started telling Dhritarashtra that you see, now the only way is that you hand over the kingdom to the Pandavas and the Pandavas, along with all the other Kaurava brothers, let them all live peacefully together as brothers. And imagine, if Duryodhana and his hundred brothers and the Pandavas are together, then who can, who can question your sovereignty, your, your ruling over the world will be unquestioned. Even the Devatas will not be able to stand against you in battle. So in this way, Bhishma, Drona, Vidura, all of them tried explaining to, explaining to Dhritarashtra.

---
### Conflict, Obstinacy, Influence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Duryodhana was unwilling. Supported by Karna, Shakuni, they were all saying, no, no, no, no, why we should give back?"
**Summary:** Despite the advice, Dhritarashtra is unable to convince Duryodhana to return the kingdom, as Duryodhana, influenced by Karna and Shakuni, refuses to yield, leading to further conflict and disagreement within the Kaurava council.

But Dhritarashtra could also see that he was a reasonable person. He could understand what they were saying. But when all of, but ultimately Dhritarashtra was bound by Duryodhana. When he looked at Duryodhana and he, he, when Dhritarashtra spoke to Duryodhana and he said, my dear son, we should take the path of peace. Let us, you please tell, we should return the kingdom to the Pandavas. Duryodhana was unwilling. Supported by Karna, Shakuni, they were all saying, no, no, no, no, why we should give back? Forget living with the Pandavas in, in friendship and all that. This is all will not work. They have actually violated the, the, the agreement and this and that, and all those kind of things. So, so Bhishma finally said, he said, Duryodhana, your intelligence has been stolen. You are, you have, you listen to only three people. One is Karna, who is a charioteer and who, who, who has, who has been cursed by even his guru. He could not even please his guru. And another is the cunning Shakuni, who is always interested in cheating. He does not follow Dharma. And the third is your small-minded brother, Dushyasana. These are the only three people you will listen to. At least listen to what we are telling you. Look at what Dronacharya is saying, Vidura is saying, I am saying, listen to your father. Why don't you follow the right path? When Karna heard this, he got very angry. He said, oh, Bhishma Deva, every time, whenever you get an opportunity, you are going on blaming me. What have I done? I have stood by Duryodhana because Duryodhana is right. What wrong has he done? He is following the path of Dharma. How can you say he is not following the path of Dharma? Like this, Duryodhana started talking. Like this, the argument went back and forth between the different personalities. Then finally, Duryodhana said that no matter what happens, I will not return the kingdom of Indraprastha to the Pandavas. I cannot live with them in friendship. We have to have a battle and we have to decide. Either they have to live or I have to live. I cannot live in peace with them. That is out of question. So like this, Duryodhana spoke and Karna immediately supported Duryodhana. Don't worry, Duryodhana. I am there. No, you let all the, let Bhishma, Drona and all the other Kauravas take care of other kings. I alone am enough for the Pandavas. All five Pandavas, I will finish. Just leave them to me. That's all. Don't worry. I am there with you.

---
### Pride, Conflict, Fate
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=1045
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "All simply boasting your talk. When the Gandharvas took away Duryodhana, what did you do? You ran away."
**Summary:** Bhishma criticizes Karna's boasting and recounts his past failures, leading Karna to vow not to fight as long as Bhishma is on the battlefield, further complicating Duryodhana's situation and highlighting the inevitability of conflict.

Bhishma looked at Karna. He said, what are you talking? All simply boasting your talk. When the Gandharvas took away Duryodhana, what did you do? You ran away. And then, Arjuna had to come and save him. And in the kingdom of Virata, you were there, we were there, we were all there and you were also there. We all saw your prowess, how you fought with Arjuna and defeated him. Actually, what happened? You ran away from Arjuna. And after doing all these, you are not even one-sixteenth of Arjuna's power. And you are saying, you will conquer all the five Pandavas. You are simply boasting all the time. Please stop this. After all, what have you done in your life? You are not even pleased, dear Guru Parashurama. You only managed to, managed to try to cheat him and he cursed you also. You have, why are you simply instigating Duryodhana like this? Now, Karna got very much ridled up and he, he became very offended. Then he stood up and he said, oh, like this, Bhishma is talking like this, always he is blaming me. I cannot tolerate this. If Bhishma is there in the battle, as long as he is in the battlefield, I will not take up weapons. If the day Bhishma Dev steps out of the battle, I will come and I will show my prowess. Like that, he stood up and he, in a huff, he walked out of the assembly. Now, Duryodhana was scratching. He said, oh my God, I was depending on Karna to fight the battle. Now Karna is left and, left and gone. Now, what will I do? Then he was seeing here and there. Then he said, okay, okay, Bhishma Dev, please cool down, calm down. Anyway, let us plan how to deal with the situation. I do not want to give back the kingdom to the Pandavas. We have to prepare for battle. And for that, I am depending upon you. You have to protect the kingdom of Srinapura. And like this, Duryodhana very diplomatically, he without, he calmed down Dronacharya and Bhishma Dev and all. And he also left the assembly. Now, so finally, it was very clear that Duryodhana would at no cost return the kingdom to the Pandavas. And Dhritarashtra, although he was advised repeatedly by Vidura, Dronacharya, Bhishma Dev, etc. Dhritarashtra could never go against what he said. What Duryodhana would say. He was a puppet in the hands of Duryodhana. Although Dhritarashtra knew the reality, he knew that the Pandavas were far too powerful. They were, they, they, no, nobody could defeat them. Karna was insignificant in front of them. Although Bhishma Dev and Dronacharya were powerful, still Arjuna had already shown in the kingdom of Virata, how he was, he was far more powerful. And he was an equal match to Bhishma Dev. An equal match to Bhishma and Dronacharya. So, Dhritarashtra knew all this and he was, he was, he was, he was very much in, grounded to reality.

---
### Destiny, Conflict, Anticipation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4Qrzow0PYu4?t=1181
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He knew that his, if there was a battle, the only outcome would be that Duryodhana and all his sons would be killed."
**Summary:** Dhritarashtra, aware of the Pandavas' strength and the likely outcome of a battle, remains a puppet of Duryodhana, sealing their fate, and the lecture concludes by teasing the Pandavas' reaction to the news of the Kauravas' unwillingness to negotiate.

He knew that his, if there was a battle, the only outcome would be that Duryodhana and all his sons would be killed. But what to do? He was a puppet in the hands of Duryodhana. He could not go against his wishes. So, it looked like his destiny was sealed. Now, this news that, Sanjaya had gone back, he had taken his proposal, and still, Duryodhana was unwilling to return the kingdom. This news reached the Pandavas. Now, when the Pandavas got the news, that the Kauravas are not going to give back their kingdom, nor are they even willing to consider their proposal, to take only five villages, even that much they are not willing to consider. What did the Pandavas do? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna.

## Rama-Rajya: A Vision of Harmony and Prosperity | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Rama-rajya, Lord Ramachandra, Chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=0

### Rama-rajya, Vaikuntha, Spiritual World

**Summary:** The speaker describes the Skanda Purana's description of Lord Ramachandra's rule and equates it to Brahmaloka or Vaikuntha, a place free from misery.

As long as Lord Ramachandra ruled over, ruled over what? Samastam Kshiti Vandalam. Kshiti means the earth. Kshiti Vandala means the earth globe. The entire earth was ruled over by Lord Ramachandra during his time. As long as Lord Ramachandra ruled over the earth, Samastam Kshiti Vandalam, the entire earth planet, Brahmaloka Samam Chakra. Lord Ramachandra's rule made the entire earth equal to Brahmaloka. Brahmaloka here refers to the spiritual world, which is where Brahman sustains the spiritual world. So it is called Brahmaloka, which is also known as Vaikuntha. The spiritual world is called Vaikuntha, meaning it is Vigatakuntha, meaning it is the place where there is no misery at all. Hence, during the reign of Lord Ramachandra...

### Rama-rajya, Ideal Society, Absence of Misery
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=114
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The pangs of old age and disease were unknown to the citizens."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the ideal conditions during Lord Ramachandra's reign, highlighting the absence of miseries, natural calamities, and premature death.

The entire earth was ruled over by Lord Ramachandra. He was rid of all kinds of miseries. There were no natural calamities. The weather was always pleasant, with the summers and winters appearing like springtime, without excessive heat or excessive cold. The earth always produced grains, fruits, and vegetables abundantly. No one ever went hungry. The pangs of old age and disease were unknown to the citizens. No son died in the present of his father, meaning everyone lived long lives and no one died young. Not only that, there was no death for anyone who did not want it. So these are all the descriptions of how Lord Ramachandra ruled over.

### Materialism, Ravana, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Despite all this economic and military and personal power, what happened? Ravana was quickly destroyed along with his life breath."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Rama-rajya with Ravana's Lanka, highlighting that economic and military power are insufficient for happiness and that Ravana was destroyed despite his wealth and power because he abducted Sita Devi.

The secret is not economic development; it is not military or political power. Ravana, in fact, had all these. His Lanka was called Svarna Lanka, why? Because every house in Lanka was cladded with gold. The richest of the people in the world today clad their houses with Italian marble or granite or some expensive stuff like that. But during Ravana's rule, every house was clad with actual gold. Can you imagine that? That was, we can imagine the economic powerhouse Lanka had become under Ravana. And moreover, the military power of Ravana was unrivaled in this whole universe. But despite all this economic and military and personal power, what happened? Ravana was quickly destroyed along with his life breath. Why? Because Dara means wife. Because he stole the wife of Lord Rama. Because Ravana abducted Sita Devi, his lifespan was cut short. And how was it cut short? Vispurjita Dhanusha. Simply by the twanging of the bow of Lord Rama, Ravana instantly lost his life. Lord Ramachandra did not have to take any big endeavor, simply by the twanging of his bow. So we can see from this that although Ravana had immense economic and military power, his citizens are not described to be free from anxiety and filled with bliss like the citizens of Lanka. And so, Ravana was cut short. And how was it cut short? Vispurjita Dhanusha.

### Kali Yuga, Chanting, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TFLSGROR_jg?t=436
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If we want real freedom from these four problems, then we have to take shelter of the holy name of Krishna and Rama."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Srimad Bhagavatam, stating that despite the faults of Kali Yuga, the chanting of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra can liberate one from material attachments and allow one to return to the spiritual world.

So the Srimad Bhagavatam says that even though Kali Yuga is filled with so many faults, so many flaws, so many problems, full of distresses, still there is one Lord. One great, good quality, auspicious quality in Kali Yuga. What is that? Keertanadeva Krishnasya. Simply by performing this Keertana or singing and chanting of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, what will happen? Keertanadeva Krishnasya Muktasangaha. One will become free from all attachments in this material world and Parambarajet, we will be able to go back to the spiritual world, which is Vaikuntha, free from all distresses, free from the problems of birth, death, disease, and old age which pervades this entire material world. Nobody is free from these four problems. If we want real freedom from these four problems, then we have to take shelter of the holy name of Krishna and Rama, we have to chant Hare Krishna Mahamantra, we have to remember Lord Ramachandra's pastimes, Lord Krishna's pastimes, and then our lives can become completely blissful, and the effects of birth, death, disease, and old age will become diminished in our life.

## Rtvik system ordered by Krishna Himself | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### Srila Prabhupada's instructions, Continuity, ISKCON history
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sheila Prabhupada wanted that whatever he had set up should continue without changes and then the movement would flourish unhindered."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines Srila Prabhupada's concerns about the ISKCON movement continuing after his departure and the three primary things he established: deity worship, disciplic conclusion, and initiations.

When Srila Prabhupada disappeared in 1977, he left behind him a glorious moment and a priceless legacy. In the days leading up to his disappearance, Srila Prabhupada was concerned specifically that the movement should not flounder in his absence. On April 10, 1977, Srila Prabhupada said, "Don't spoil it. We are very good, prestigious people. Don't spoil it. It's a hard day. It started with a very emotional moment. I am in humble condition, I am just comfortable, such exalted position. Don't spoil it. That is my request. Increase. That will depend on your character, behavior, position." Srila Prabhupada repeated the same request on April 22, 1977, saying, "Keep to our principle and see our GVP. It's very a lot. Then everything is gone. I am not there. Do that. That is my request. Whatever I left to you, I have taught you. Follow that. Nobody will be able to do it. No māyā will tell you. Now Kṛṣṇa is giving us, and there is no scarcity of money. To cut both, I'm sorry. Everything is there. You have got good sages all over the world. You have got, in the street you have got… Even if I die suddenly, you'll be able to manage. That I want. Manage nicely, don't go back." That was Prabhupada's concern. Again, Sheila Prabhupada reiterated the same concern on 29th April 1977 where he said, "The only request is don't spoil it." Once more, Sheila Prabhupada on 24th of May 1977 said the same thing. Sheila Prabhupada wanted that whatever he had set up should continue without changes and then the movement would flourish unhindered. Out of the many things that Sheila Prabhupada set up in ISKCON, three things were primary. One was the system of deity worship. Everything that was offered to the deities, whether the arathis or the boga, would have to be first offered to the guru Sheila Prabhupada and then to Nithyananda Prabhu. After that, then the arathi or the boga would be offered to Goranga Maha Prabhu, then it would be offered to Radharani, and finally it would get offered to Krishna. This was the system of deity worship Sheila Prabhupada set up. The second thing was the disciplic conclusion, which was all the deities were offered to the guru and then there in Prabhupada's books in their pure and unadulterated form. And the third thing was the system of deeksha or initiations Sheila Prabhupada set up and ran the dithích system of initiations in ISKCON from 1970 to 1977 and on July 9th 1977 Sheila Prabhupada ordered the dithích system to continue even in his absence. All that was twelve years and the existence of riti groups or in口ic scoring groups and their duasas all that was first mentioned and his mothers brothers and sisters linked gifts all that was all that was reduced to satan as per tradition means he was requesting a nonpublical organization then on 3rd he went to India in 1971 under carbohydrates heComputed a memorialitta needed was that nothing be changed in ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's departure, especially these three things, dictatorship, books and initiations.

The GBC started changing everything. They changed the system of Deity worship to offering everything to Krishna directly first, whether it was Bhoga or it was Arati. This was a complete tectonic shift in the method of Deity worship Prabhupada taught. Not only that, they changed the books and they changed the system of initiations. They changed all the rules of the GBC. They changed all the three primary things in ISKCON. Srila Prabhupada had warned against this many times, as Srila Prabhupada said in this letter to Bhakta Dasa written on 24th of November 1974. Srila Prabhupada wrote, "Don't change from this to that. That is your American disease. This is very serious that you always want to change everything." Despite Srila Prabhupada's warnings and concerns, he did not change anything. He did not change anything. He did not change anything. After Srila Prabhupada's departure, the GBC went ahead and changed all the three primary things set up by Srila Prabhupada.

### GBC, Zonal Acharya System, Contradiction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the GBC's resolutions regarding the appointment of spiritual masters, highlighting a contradiction between their 1978 declaration that Srila Prabhupada appointed eleven Diksha Gurus and their 1999 acceptance that they themselves created the zonal acharya system.

As far as initiations go, on March 19, 1978, in Resolution No. 16, the GBC resolved. The GBC will consider each year at Gaurapurnima, the appointment of new spiritual masters to be approved by a three-fourth vote. However, for 1978, the GBC will consider each year at Gaurapurnima, the appointment of new spiritual masters to be approved by a three-fourth vote. However, for 1978, no new spiritual masters shall be appointed other than the eleven selected by Srila Prabhupada. So, the GBC declared in 1978 that Srila Prabhupada appointed eleven Diksha Gurus. However, later in 1999, the GBC resolved in Resolution No. 403 that, unfortunately, shortly after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC body allowed for an unauthorized zonal acharya system to evolve in ISKCON, elevating eleven exclusive successor Diksha Gurus. So, in 1999, 21 years after their resolution declaring that Srila Prabhupada selected eleven Diksha Gurus, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila Prabhupada, who would be appointed as the first Diksha Gurus. So, the GBC did a old fast and accepted that it was they, not Srila follows that all other Diksha Gurus as well as the system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON by the GBC indicating no objection are all invented by the GBC.

### GBC, Diksha Guru System, Lack of Precedent
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=460
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Satswaroop Prabhu openly accepted that they, the GBC, did not know what they were doing in instituting the Diksha Guru system in ISKCON in 1978."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Satswaroop Prabhu, one of the original 11 Diksha Gurus, admitting that the GBC did not know what they were doing when instituting the Diksha Guru system and that there was no scriptural precedent for it.

Till date, the GBC has not given any explanation for the source of their system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON. In fact, Satswaroop Prabhu, one of the original 11 Diksha Gurus from 1978, said in the year 1986, in an article which appeared in the September-October edition of Hinduism Today, in the article titled, Murder quickens wider crisis in Krishna sect. In this article, Satswaroop Prabhu said, "If the different worship systems we instituted were accepted, then the system of regulating Diksha Gurus in ISKCON would not have been excessive. My answer is that we did not know what to do because there was no precedent scriptural rule which told us what to do." Satswaroop Prabhu openly accepted that they, the GBC, did not know what they were doing in instituting the Diksha Guru system in ISKCON in 1978. Shaila Prabhupad did not order them to institute a Diksha Guru system in ISKCON nor did he give any instructions on what to do in such a system. Nor did the GBC model their Diksha Guru system on any precedent or tradition or scripture or shastra. It appears that they did what appeared right as per their collective intelligence and discrimination. Their intelligence and discrimination decreased when the JVC was instilled into the Diksha discrimination dictated that the Ritvik system that Srila Prabhupada ordered for implementation in ISKCON on July 9, 1977, had to be discarded after Srila Prabhupada disappeared.

### Srila Prabhupada, Krishna, Leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=715
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Basis, just to see whether he is qualified. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces an exchange between Srila Prabhupada and Mr. Kevin Lehart about how Srila Prabhupada chose leaders, temple presidents, and devotees for BBT positions.

Now, interestingly, there is an exchange between Srila Prabhupada and one Mr. Kevin Lehart, which was recorded on 14th of July 1976. Listen to this carefully as Srila Prabhupada explains how he appointed temple presidents, how he appointed devotees to BBT positions, etc. How do you choose? You are responsible for the organization. Yes. Are you the one who chooses, who runs each center, who is responsible in each temple? Yes. Our program is to open centers in every village, every town to Prabhupada and Krishnapada. And you choose the leaders? Yes. What Krishna does, he tells you, how does that work? No. I have been chosen by my spiritual master. And you in turn choose others? Yes. That is our succession. On what basis? Basis on the order of Krishna. Originally, the order is from Krishna. How does that come? It comes by disciplic succession. Just like Krishna, Krishna said this knowledge to Arjuna, and Arjuna said to others, and it is open. Everyone can take it. There is no secrecy. We have to accept order. That's all. I missed that. I am sorry. The order is already there. It is open, open secret. There is no secrecy. Anyone can take it. All right. But in terms of specific, say, choosing specific things, specific details. Yes. In all details. How to become Krishna Karsayas, there is all details in this book. Bhagavad Gita as it is. So he is asking what is a man's qualification to be chosen? In other words, Prabhupada will decide who should be leader if he is qualified. And there is a process for making him qualified. And there is a process for testing to see if he is qualified. So in that way, it all comes from Krishna. You chose, for example, these fellows here to run the publishing house and be responsible for... Yes. Like he is in charge of publication. He is in charge of doing his research. On what basis, sir? Can you tell me some of the things? Basis, just to see whether he is qualified. That's all. Just like ordinarily, when manager is appointed, by the superior authority, on the merit, on his qualification, that's all.

### Krishna, Spiritual Master, Direct Communication
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=885
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "God speaks to you when you are qualified. You cannot expect God as order supplier."
**Summary:** The conversation continues, focusing on whether the choice of leaders is a mediated choice or a direct communication from Krishna, with Srila Prabhupada emphasizing that God speaks to those who are qualified.

Okay. It's not... It's a mediated choice, or is it a direct communication from Krishna? That's my question. No. Oh, he is asking whether we claim that God speaks to us directly. Yes. God speaks to you when you are qualified. You cannot expect God as order supplier. Yes. When He speaks to you, you are qualified. You cannot expect God as order supplier. You cannot see that you are qualified. He is strict with you. But if God, the spiritual master, is the representative of God to disciple, because he is in direct contact with God. Yes, yes. My spiritual master appointed me, then you do. Similarly, I shall appoint somebody else. This is your choice. It's difficult for people to understand that God can speak to a man. That is… They question, how can God… how can God speak to some man? That is… Anything that the radio message is coming, a foolish man cannot understand how it is coming. He'll think how it is there, speaking. So any foolish man will be astonished how things are happening. That is foolishness. But God says, yes, please, I know this. What? Author? Saivayam Teja? No, no, no. buddhi-yogaṁ dadāmi tam yena mām abhyayanti teṣyāṁ sattva-yogaṁ tam yena mām abhyayanti dadāmi buddhi-yogaṁ tam yena mām abhyayanti Yes. To those who are constantly devoted and worship me with love, I give them the understanding by which they can come to me. God is situated in everyone's… As soon as he sees that here is a qualified person, then he gives him instruction. But in the same way that Kṛṣṇa says he will provide for all your needs, you still must work to achieve whatever Kṛṣṇa is giving you. You work for Kṛṣṇa. You have to work to get your necessities. In the same way, I am curious with respect to the way Kṛṣṇa communicates with you, whether it's in a similar kind of way that gives you your necessities. In other words, when you decide that someone is to be in charge of a particular temple, does Kṛṣṇa tell you that this person should be in charge? You, by judging him, say this person is qualified. Yes. Because a devotee always concerns Kṛṣṇa, and he gives order. So that's a more direct communication. Yes. And he gives order.

### Krishna, Intelligence, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=1082
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee always consults Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa tells him, do like this, not figuratively, practically."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada clarifies that a devotee always consults Krishna directly, and Krishna gives practical instructions, correcting Ramesvara Prabhu's interpretation that intelligence alone is sufficient.

Because intelligence… Our philosophy is that intelligence comes from Kṛṣṇa. So if I have something… Your philosophy is that your daily necessities come from Kṛṣṇa. Yes. I understand. Suppose my intelligence sees that this person is qualified, that means Kṛṣṇa has told me. No, not necessarily. Kṛṣṇa will tell directly. A devotee always concerns Kṛṣṇa, and Kṛṣṇa tells him. Two levels. Not figuratively. Practically. Does that apply then to other kinds of decisions and other kinds of activities as well? Everything. Everything. Because a devotee… A devotee does not do anything without concerning Kṛṣṇa. But that applies to a very greatly elevated soul. That is not an ordinary person. And that is… Therefore the minor devotees, they concern the spiritual master. That is our process. Yasya prasādād bhagavata-prasādād. I see. I was trying to explain the minor devotees. No, I was talking about… You were talking about the topmost level. Yes. That's a good phrase for the service. That's it. That will do it for me. I thank you kindly. Now, in this exchange, Śrīla Prabhupāda, on being questioned by Mr. Kevin Lehart, about how he appointed temple presidents and devotees to BBT positions, Rāmēśvara Prabhu interpreted Śrīla Prabhupāda's answer by saying, suppose my intelligence sees that this person is qualified, that means Kṛṣṇa has told me. This was Rāmēśvara Prabhu's interpretation. In effect, Rāmēśvara Prabhu said that the decision was made by using one's intelligence and that meant that Kṛṣṇa had ordered it. In other words, he said that there is no direct consultation with Kṛṣṇa. But Śrīla Prabhupāda immediately corrected him saying, no, not necessarily. He said that Kṛṣṇa will tell directly. A devotee always consults Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa tells him, do like this, not figuratively, practically. That means even to appoint temple president or any devotee to a BBT post, Śrīla Prabhupāda would always consult Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa was telling directly to Śrīla Prabhupāda, Śrīla Prabhupāda would always consult Kṛṣṇa and Kṛṣṇa was telling directly to Śrīla Prabhupāda, do like this, practically, not figuratively. Meaning, Kṛṣṇa was telling Śrīla Prabhupāda directly whom to appoint and Śrīla Prabhupāda would appoint such a person as a temple president or to any official post in the BBT.

---
### Diksha Guru, Krishna, Ritviks
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6nmzI3Tjkvc?t=1213
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In other words, all appointments in ISKCON came form Krishna and Śrīla Prabhupāda being the Vāyā media relayed about it."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that if Srila Prabhupada consulted Krishna for even minor appointments, it is even more applicable to the appointment of Diksha Gurus, and that all appointments in ISKCON came from Krishna.

Remember the verse Śrīla Prabhupāda so often quoted? Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. Āchāryam mām vijāniyām nāvamanyetakarhi chit. These are Krishna's words. Whatever is said by the spiritual master is to be taken as directly the order of Krishna. No one must disrespect or disobey the spiritual master's order because the spiritual master's order is the order of Krishna. Now imagine, if even for BBT posts and temple president posts, Śrīla Prabhupāda was consulting Krishna, directly and Krishna was ordering whom to appoint, then is it not all the more applicable to appointing Diksha Gurus in ISKCON, particularly for the time after Śrīla Prabhupāda's departure? Diksha Guru position is far higher than that of a temple president or even a GVC. So it is natural that if Śrīla Prabhupāda was always consulting Krishna directly for appointing a devotee to any post, then naturally, Śrīla Prabhupāda wouldn't appoint anyone to Diksha Guru post, without consulting Krishna directly. In other words, all appointments in ISKCON came form Krishna and Śrīla Prabhupāda being the Vāyā media relayed about it. that order from Krishna. Now comes the question. If this is the case, then how do you think Srila Prabhupada appointed the Ritwiks to carry on initiations in ISKCON after his departure? How does the GBC think Srila Prabhupada sent out to all the temple presidents and the GBCs the July 9, 1977 order for implementing the Ritwiks system of initiations in ISKCON? How does the GBC think Srila Prabhupada decided on this order? Do you think Srila Prabhupada simply used this intelligence and discrimination like the GBC did in either inventing the no-objection guru system or the GBC even today is doing in selecting diksha gurus? Do you think Srila Prabhupada did not consult Krishna?

---
