So, Srila Prabhupada mentions in this, if you do not take your initiation from the Sampradaya, then it is useless. Now, there are so many Appa Sampradaya. They do not come in discipling succession but becomes Guru, teacher. Therefore, everything is topsy-turvy. Nobody has got fixed idea what is God. Everyone has created his own philosophy. The whole thing is now confused. So, as we can see in the above quote, initiation must be taken from the Sampradaya. That means the Guru who gives the initiation must be a person who is part of the Sampradaya or who is in the Sampradaya or Guru Parampara. Now, in another quote Prabhupada says, otherwise one becomes Guru. Anyone, just like nowadays they become, they are manufacturing Guru. Guru is not manufactured. Guru is in the discipling succession. Guru is in the discipling succession. One who strictly follows the footsteps of the Shadgo Swamis. This is from the lecture in the Srimad Bhagavatam. In another place, Prabhupada says, our Guru or spiritual master comes down from that discipling succession. It is not that, that somebody all of a sudden becomes Guru and manufactures his own philosophy. We don't accept such nonsense. We must accept somebody who is actually bona fide coming in discipling succession, not others. And yet another place, Prabhupada says, one should take initiation from a bona fide spiritual master coming in the discipling succession who is authorized by his predecessor spiritual master. This is called Diksha Vidhana.

---
### Disciplic Succession, Guru Parampara, Diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=900
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Diksha taken from such a Guru results in taking the disciple back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that a Guru must be one of the 32 personalities in the Guru Parampara and that Diksha from such a Guru leads back to Godhead. He also states that a Guru should not initiate if they cannot take the disciple back to Godhead.

So, in this last quote, Prabhupada makes it clear that Guru is in the discipling succession. And in other places, Prabhupada says, the Guru comes down from that discipling succession. In the discipling succession or from the discipling succession means that the Guru has to be one of the 32 personalities mentioned in the Guru Parampara. It cannot be otherwise. Diksha taken from such a Guru results in taking the disciple back to Godhead. So, Prabhupada says in the Srimad Bhagavatam, according to Shastra, the duty of the Guru is to take the disciple back to Godhead. If he is unable to do so and instead hinders the disciple in going back to Godhead, he should not be a Guru. In other words, from this quote, unless the Guru has the capability to take the disciple back to Godhead, he should not give initiation. Thus, we can clearly see that one process of liberation for a conditioned soul involves Diksha from an Acharya in the Guru Parampara, while the other process of appointment of a successor Acharya does not necessarily involve Diksha. Both processes are unrelated. One is the process of appointment of a Guru and the other is the appointment of a disciple. Both are separate, independent and cannot be compared. We cannot apply the principles of appointment of a Guru to those of the appointment of a disciple or vice versa. Hence, irrespective of whether our Parampara is Diksha Parampara or Shiksha Parampara,

---
### Rithvik System, GBC, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=994
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "No one can become Guru in ISKCON without the specific order of Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that no one can be a Guru in ISKCON without Prabhupada's order and that the Rithvik system should be followed. He criticizes the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai for creating a self-appointed Guru system and contradicting Prabhupada.

no one can become Guru in ISKCON without the specific order of Srila Prabhupada. And such a Diksha Guru must necessarily be a pure devotee, thirty-third in the Parampara. And as we all know, Srila Prabhupada never ordered anyone to become Diksha Guru. Hence, we have to follow the order of Srila Prabhupada given in the July 9th, 1977 Diksha Parampara. And this directive is to be followed in ISKCON to avoid the violation of the principles of Vaishnava Siddhanta. Now, one more important point we must remember is that if we were to accept the argument of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur being appointed by Bipin Bihari Goswami, then the question arises that why should the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group limit the Diksha Gurus to only disciples of Srila Prabhupada, or the disciples of their self-appointed Gurus? Bipin Bihari Goswami was not in the Parampara. So, someone can argue that we can bring in Vaishnavas or devotees from outside ISKCON and declare him also to be a Diksha Guru in ISKCON. Why limit it to only devotees within ISKCON? So, we can see that their argument to justify their self-appointed Guru system can only create more problems in the ISKCON. The GBC of ISKCON Mumbai has created this self-appointed Guru system out of thin air without any reference to Sadhu, Shastra or Guru. They have contradicted Srila Prabhupada and in some cases even changed Srila Prabhupada's instructions to fit their concocted no-objection Guru system. Actually, if you want a proof of this, in the original Chaitanya Charita Amrita Adi Leela Chapter 1 Summary,

### Book Changes, GBC, Spiritual Harm
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=1113
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The GBC has the audacity to change Prabhupada's books, to contradict him, to find fault with Prabhupada's words."
**Summary:** The speaker provides evidence of the GBC changing Prabhupada's books to fit their self-appointed Guru system and describes the spiritual harm caused to devotees by unqualified Gurus.

Srila Prabhupada said this, which we mentioned at the beginning of this discussion. Srila Vishwanatha Chakravarthi Thakur accepted Srila Jagannath Das Babaji, who initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who in turn initiated Srila Gaurakeshwar Das Babaji, the spiritual master of Om Vishnupada, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj, the divine master of our humble self. So, this did not fit into their self-appointed no-objection Guru system. So, what did they do? They changed Prabhupada's books to Srila Vishwanatha Chakravarthi Thakur accepted Srila Jagannath Das Babaji, the spiritual master of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who in turn accepted, replaced the word initiated with accepted, Srila Gaurakeshwar Das Babaji, the spiritual master of Om Vishnupada, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj, the divine master of our humble self. So, we can see how the GBC's concoction is playing havoc with the society and the lives of innocent devotees. The GBC has the audacity to change Prabhupada's books, to contradict him, to find fault with Prabhupada's words. And because of their actions, so many innocent devotees' spiritual lives have been destroyed. Because this GBC thrust upon them, without their consent or knowledge, a neophyte devotee as their Diksha Guru. And subsequently, when the neophyte Guru fell down, thousands of devotees who were initiated by him had to suffer spiritual, mental and emotional trauma and agony.

### Guru, Krishna Consciousness, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=1219
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who has understood Krishna as the supreme controller, the supreme person, the supreme maintainer, and everything, the supreme ultimate, then he can become Guru. Otherwise, don't try to become Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Prabhupada to emphasize that only someone who fully understands Krishna can be a Guru and that conditioned souls cannot be Gurus.

Srila Prabhupada said in a lecture, Therefore, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu confirms, Ae Krishna Tattva Veta Sai Guru Haya. One who has understood Krishna as the supreme controller, the supreme person, the supreme maintainer, and everything, the supreme ultimate, then he can become Guru. Otherwise, don't try to become Guru. Impersonalist, half understood, partially understood, he cannot become Guru. This is the Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's formula. Therefore, first of all, try to understand Krishna. You will understand in such a way that you can refute all others' argument, all others' opposition. There are so many opposing elements. Then you are Guru. Otherwise, you cannot become a Guru. Guru is not so tiny thing or trifle thing that everyone becomes Guru Maharaj. No, that is not Guru. Samahatma Vasudevaha Sarvamiti Samahatma Sudurlabha. So, you heard what Prabhupada said. Half understood, partially understood, he cannot become Guru. Only an Uttamaadhikari Guru in the Guru Parampara can claim to know full and not partial. Conditioned souls cannot know fully. They can only know partially. In other words, conditioned souls cannot become Guru. To become a Guru and to give Diksha is not a trifle thing. It is an extremely serious affair because Guru has to take the disciple back to Godhead.

### Rithvik System, GBC, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=1304
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without a direct order from Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON's self-appointed no objection Gurus are not in the Guru Parampara."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that there is no evidence Prabhupada ordered the GBC's Guru nomination system, that the system is unauthorized, and that the Rithvik system is the authorized process for taking initiation from Prabhupada.

So, there is not a single shred of evidence that Srila Prabhupada ordered Gurus to be nominated with a no objection indicated by the GBC body. How long will the GBC stubbornly refuse to accept the truth and keep coming up with various concoctions just to fulfill their megalomaniacal ambition to become Diksha Guru? How long will the GBC continue to slaughter innocent devotees' spiritual lives to quench their thirst for honour, prestige and worship? Without a direct order from Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON's self-appointed no objection Gurus are not in the Guru Parampara. Thus, the Guru nomination system of ISKCON is unauthorized, while the Rithvik system authorized by Srila Prabhupada in writing via the July 9, 1977 Directive, fulfills all the conditions stipulated for a devotee to take initiation from a Mahabhagavata in the Guru Parampara and go back to Godhead. The Rithvik process is independent of whether the Guru Parampara is Siksha or Diksha Parampara. Whether the Guru Parampara is Siksha or Diksha Parampara, has no relevance to the process of taking Diksha from Srila Prabhupada via the Rithvik system personally instituted in ISKCON by Srila Prabhupada. Thus, the Rithvik system does not violate any principle, even if our Guru Parampara is considered to be based on Siksha and not Diksha. All devotees are requested to reject the self-appointed no objection Guru system promoted by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai. Instead, please take shelter of the Rithvik system ordered by Srila Prabhupada on July 9, 1977 and followed by the GBC of ISKCON Bangalore. Hare Krishna, Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai!
